title: Receipt for grain taxes
summary: This ostracon bears a Greek receipt issued by the royal granary and two subscriptions, one in Greek by the sitologos (on the left) and one in Demotic (on the right).
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 19 25 (English) (Greek) Year 6, Mesore 7. X son of Harpochrates has paid for (the tax of) 5/6 artaba (per aroura) for - - - for the same year: one artaba of wheat and a half = 1 ½ (artabas) of wheat. Y sitologos (Demotic) The receipt of the royal granary: 1 ½ (artabas) of wheat for the Southern Island, for year 6.

title: Receipt for ennomion
summary: This ostracon contains a receipt for ἐννόμιον.
translation_text: Greek: Year 12, Pauni 16. For the ἐννόμιον: Ptolemaios, son of Sabbataios, one and a half drachma, total 1,5. ... Demotic: Has written: Petemestous, son of Pete-...

title: Receipt for monthly tax
summary: This difficult-to-read ostracon seems to follow the formula of the monthly taxes of an unknown type described by Uebel (1969)

title: Granary receipt
summary: This ostracon contains a receipt for grain from the thesaurus.

title: Granary receipt
summary: This ostracon contains a granary receipt with two payments on it.

title: Receipt for dike and bath tax
summary: This ostracon contains a receipt for dike and bath tax.

title: Tax receipt
summary: This ostracon is a receipt for dike and bath tax, payed by the same person as in O. Leid. 383 and 384: Πεσούριος Ψανσνῶτος.

title: Receipt for dyke-tax
summary: Receipt for the payment of Peteamenophis for the dyke-money
translation_text: (Translation from P. L. Bat. 19) [1] What Peteamenophis, son of N.N., has paid to the bank for the dyke-money [2] of year 2: 1 stater, (1) kite (and) 4 obols, with an extra charge at the rate of 2,5 obols (per stater). Written in year 3 [3] of Gaius (l.p.h.) Caesar (l.p.h.) [4] who is august, Germanicus (l.p.h.) [5] the august month, day 13. Signed, Pikos son of Kephalos.

title: Receipt for dyke-tax
summary: Receipt for the payment of Poregebthis for the dyke-money.
translation_text: (Translation from P. L. Bat. 19) [1] What Poregebthis, son of Onnophris, has paid to the bank for the dyke-money of year 1: [2] 1 stater and (1) kite (and) 4 obols / making 1,5 kite (and) 2 obols / making 1 stater and (1) kite (and) 4 obols again, with an extra charge [3] at the rate of 2,5 obols (per stater). Written in year 2 of Tiberius (l.p.h.) Caesar (l.p.h.) [4] who is august, Choiach, the august day. Signed, Kephalos, son of Epikrates.

title: Receipt for isw-tax
summary: Receipt for isw-tax
translation_text: (Translation from P. L. Bat. 23) 1. Year 4, Epeiph, (day) 24. From Pꜣ-šr-pꜣ-wr, son of Ḥr-tꜣj=f-nḫṱ: 2. 1/3 deben as the isw-money of year 4. Signed, Ḥr, son of Pꜣ-ḫl-Ḫnsw. 3. Signed, Ḏḥwṱ-i.ir-dj.t-s: 1/3 deben as the isw-money. Year 4, Epeiph, (day) 24.

title: Business Letter on an Ostracon
summary: Business letter from Paulinus to Serenus, whom he addresses as his brother.
translation_text: (Translation from P. L. Bat. 23) [1] Paulinus to his brother Serenus, [ - - greetings.] [2] In conformity with what you have written to me, brother, [ [3] I have done my best, and I have obtained, in conformity with [ [4] for four artabas fi[ve (?) [5] the price thereof [I (?)] did not - - [ [6] of four artabas 4 drachmas polite [ - - . Let me know (?)] [7] if in any other way you have need. Greetings [to/from [8] I pray for your health.

title: Account of the Lesonis-Priest
summary: The text contains an account of a lesonis-priest
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 23] 1. The account of the mr šn. The mr šn: 14 (deben); the scribe of the wꜥb-priests: 4 deben; 2. the rest of the (?) ..., to (the value of) 5 deben: ... 23. Classification: 3. Hrj=w: (commodities?) to (the value of) 4 deben; Pꜣ-šr-pꜣ-ẖrṱ (?), son of Ns-...-iw: ⸢...⸣ grain (?): 4. making 7 deben; the rest: 16 (deben). 5. (art. of) barley: 2 1/2; (art. of) barley: 4 1/2 ... [ (?) ] 6. (art. of barley: 1 1/2; (art. of) barley: 1 ⸢1/4⸣ [ (?) ] 7. (art. of) wheat (?): 10+ (?) 8. 2; (art. of) barley: 1 (?)

title: List of names
summary: A list of names of an unknown provenance in three parts, connected with the registration of commodities(?). For every person a patronymic and a metronymic are given (except in 1. 7).

title: Receipt of payment for grain
summary: Receipt of three artabas of wheat, paid by Phib (and Patricius?) and signed by the elder Isac.
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 25 76] 1 “(cross) Phib the camel-driver and Patricius (?) from Mikron (have paid) 3 artabas of wheat for the xth indiction.” “Isac the elder. Indiction 6.”

title: Building account
summary: An account of expenses for building repairs, which is probably arranged chronologically. A working party was 'demolishing' the ancient structures of the building.

title: Declaration on Oath
summary: Declaration on oath regarding the property of at least two ladies.

title: Lease of fishing rights
summary: The upper part of a lease of fishing rights, in which two inhabitants of Oxyrhynchus offer to undertake the 'fishing' in a part of the nome. The offer of the lease is submitted to three contractors of fishing rights.
translation_text: [From P. L. Bat. 25 60] [1] To the Aurelii Sarapion, son of Aphynchis, and - - -, son of - - -, and Harpocration, son of Nilus, the three contractors of the fishing tax [of the city (and) the Oxyrhynchite nome], from the Aurelii Ammonius, son of Sarapammon, and - - -, son of - - -, both from the glorious city of the Oxyrhynchites. [5] Of our own volition we offer to lease for - - - the right to fish in the waters left by the past Nile flood - - - in the Oxyrhynchite nome, starting from - - -.

title: Receipt of advance payment for vegetable seed
summary: A receipt of an unspecified amount of money, which will be later repaid in the form of four artabas of vegetable seed.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 25 (English) (1) (1st hand) - - - apart from other things that Hatres owes the same person on the basis of another contract. Subscriber of the acknowledging party is Melanas, son of Menches, about x years of age, with a scar - -. (5) (2nd hand) I Hatres, son of - - -, of Persian descent, acknowledge having received from Marcus Antonius Nuagros (?), belonging to the discharged (soldiers), the price of four artabas of new and pure vegetable seed, (measured) with the sic-choenix dromos-measure of my brother Orsenouphis, which (artabas) I shall deliver (10) in the month of Soterios of the current year – apart from other things that I owe the same person on the basis of another contract-, as stated above. Melanas, son of Menches, has written for him, because he is illiterate. (3rd hand) I, Marcus Antonius Nuagros (?), have given the price of 4 artabas as stated above. (15) (4th hand) (Registered on the xth month - -) of the fifteenth year of Emperor Caesar Domitianus Augustus Germanicus.

title: Tax receipt
summary: Receipt for the payment of 1 solidus less 3 carats for the gratuity of a certain official.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 77 (English) (1) (1st hand) (Chi-Rho) (Paid for the?) assessment of the eight indiction of te - - - through the representative board of the village, for the gravity of the - - - - - -: one gold solidus less three carats, total 1 gld. sol. less 3 c. in all, through the representatives of the village. They agree. Isakis has written on their behalf in the 8th indiction. (Chi-Rho) (5) (2nd hand) Peeus, tax-collector, agrees. (3rd hand) Josepis agrees. (4th hand) Phibios agrees. (5th hand) Joseph

title: Loan of money
summary: A loan of money - two golden coins - for two persons, which the persons will repay the first day of the next month of the current year.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 13 1 (1) After the consulate of the most illustrious Flavii Vincomalus and Opilio at the - - - of the 8th indiction. The Aurelii [N.N.], son of Parion and his son Parion of the (5) same city greet [N.N.], the most eminent son of Diogenes of the illustrious and most illustrious city of Oxyrhynchus. We acknowledge under mutual security that we have received directly in our hand from you for our own and necessary use two golde coins, unalloyed, current, of the right weight and valid, sum total 2 golden coins, on condition that we shall give as interest thereof the legal 1% a month. (10) We shall necessarily repay those coins on the first day of the next month of the current year - - -.

title: Memorandum and list of documents
summary: On the recto, a man states that he has received a contract concerning a plot of land in the Hermopolite nome. The verso bears a list of documents of different persons.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 27 (English) Recto (1st hand) - - - to Theon (?) Zopyrus greets the most honorable Theon. I have received the deed for the 1 ½ ¼ arouras for - - - and - - - being part part of the kleroi of Isizelmis and Zazelmis. Its neighbours: to the south, Claudia Heraclia and others; to the north and east, Iason, son of Ptolemaeus, and others; to the west, a mound. Tesenptollis from the village of Thmoinetei in the Cynopolite nome, and Sambion, son of Dositheus, whose mother is Demarion, from Psychis in the (district) Pasko in the Hermopolite nome. The scribe’s fee is yours, as you wish. Verso [Notes by Theon] (2nd hand) An order from Thoonis, accountant of Tiberius Claudius - - -, by Rodocles the steward of Lacholos in the upper district of the Hermopolite nome. A contract of Apoll. . ., son of Posidonius the Elder, grandson of Ptoll..., whose mother is Isidora, of (the village of) Ptemenkurkis (?), by - - -. An order of Sarap. . . the elder, nicknamed Shorty (?), without father (?) - - -, for 8 drachmas. ------------------------------------------ Draw up a - - - document (?) for Sarap. . . son of Diogenes, grandson of Phaminus, elder of (the village of) Ares (?) in the district Patre (?) - - - district - - -.

title: Sale of wine for future delivery
summary: A receipt of payment for 125 jars of new, indigenous wine. The price had been set with another person.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 13 2 (English) - - -] we Aurelius Makarios, son of Makarios (?) and Aurelius Poimen son of Phoibammon(?) and Aurelius Sabinus, son of Johannes, tenants, of the same place greet [N.N.]. We acknowledge under mutual security that we have received from your brillancy the between each other agreed and stipulated price of one hundred and twenty-five jars of new, indigenous and excellent wine, each jar containing seven doubles, (each double containing) six measures, while your brillancy supplies the jars, sum total 125 jars each containing 7 doubles, (each double containing) six measures. Those we shall necessarily deliver in the month Mesore of the harvest of the xth indiction D.V.

title: List of names and Abstracts of transactions
summary: On the recto could have been an account. On the verso might be a transaction concerning catoecic land.

title: Seed list
summary: Recto: a list of 'prosodou' and land of the king with a report of the amount of seed corn. Verso: some letters.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 28 (English) Sansneus, son of Sansneus, grandson of Mysthes (?) - - - for ¾ 1/32 arouras of revenue land, ½ 1/16 artabas of wheat and - - - artabas of barley (?) Orseus, son of Sansneus, grandson of Mysthes - - - for ½ 1/16 1/64 of revenue land, 1/3 1/8 artabas of wheat - - - Sintamis (?), daughter of Phasis, granddaughter if Horu, represented by - - - and Orsenouphis, son of Orseus, grandson of Stotoetis (?) - - - Heron, son of an unknown father, whose mother is Soteris - - - Sotas, son of Sotas, son of Orseus - - - for ¼ 1/8 1/16 1/32 1/64 arouras of royal land, 1 1/x artabas of wheat for seed - - - - - - son of Chiasis, grandson of Chiasis - - - son of Tepheros, grandson of Sansneus - - - for seed - - - Seleucus, son of an unknown father, whose mother is - - - Pharus (?), son of an unknown father, whose mother is - - - for seed - - - Eubius, son of an unknown father, whose mother is - - -

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: The letter is a private letter of which the meaning remains obscure.

title: Memorandum
summary: The text contains requests or demands regarding various financial matters.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for a payment in money as well as in kind.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 78 (English) (1st hand) - - - thirty-four gold solidi, total gld sol. 34, as well as eighty artabas of wheat (measured) with the measure of The-, together this totals 80 art. wheat and 34 gld. sil. in all. Makarios, stenographer and scribe, agrees. (2nd hand) (cross) Daniel agrees. (3rd hand) (cross) Makare agrees.

title: Contract
summary: This papyrus is a part of a private contract between Aurelius Paulus and Aurelius Theon.

title: Land list by tenants and cultivation
summary: A list of land, with agricultural products, arranged by tenants, including amounts of arouras and artabas.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 48 (English) (1st hand) By tenant - - - (sown) with wheat 4 1/16 1/64 arouras (sown) with flax 1/8 1/32 aroura (sown) with barley ½ 1/8 1/16 aroura By Kalthon (?) (sown) with flax ¼ aroura By . . . . .is (sown) with wheat 5 arouras of pasture 4 1/16 arouras (sown) with flax 1 ¼ 1/8 1/16 1/64 aroura (sown) with barley ½ ¼ 1/8 1/16 1/32 arouras Total 16 ½ 1/8 1/32 arouras (2nd hand) By tenant Psoïs (3rd hand) Total: of wheat 59 artabas and 8 choenices of barley 23 artabas and 1 choenix in half: of wheat 29 ½ artabas and 4 choenices of barley 11 ½ artabas

title: Fragment of a petition
summary: This petition (or possibly a memorandum of court proceedings) deals with family matters.

title: Receipt for rent
summary: The text is a receipt for the rent of some land. Only a partial payment is concerned.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 37 (English) Isidorus alias Aclepiades, ex-gymnasiarch, represented by Aelianus, greets Papus and his brothers. I have received from you on account of rents as partial payment eighty-four artabas of wheat = 84 art. of wheat, without prejudice to my claim on the other artabas of rents you owe me. In the 19th year of the Aurelii Antoninus and Commodus, the imperial lords, (month day).

title: Abstracts of contracts
summary: The text consist of abstracts of contracts, with personal names and expressions referring to contracts.

title: Plato, Phaedo I: 65 A8-C3, II: 65 E3 - 66 B3
summary: This papyrus contains several lines of the Phaedo, written by Plato.

title: Tax list arranged by villages
summary: A tax list, with amounts that represent the amount of taxes mentioned by the χωρία.

title: Loan of money
summary: It contains the main part of a loan between two women, each accompanied by her guardian.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 13 3 (English) In the eighteenth year of Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, on the seventh month of Tybi, in Tebtunis in the Polemon division in the Arsinoite nome. Tapeteeus, daughter of Peteeus, son of Domnos from the village of Kerkesouchon Oros about 48 years old, unscarred (?), in the presence of her brother Mysthes, her guardian, about 36 years of age with a scar on - - - - - agrees with Theapynchis, fatherless and daughter of [N.N.] about . . years of age, unscarred, in the presence of her guardian, her relation [N.N.], son of Poseidonius with a scar on his right (?) eyebrow, that she has received from her, Thenapynchis, promptly and immediately a loan of three hundred and eighty drachmas at a total interest of one drachma per mina per month, which Tapeteeus will repay Thenapynchis in the month of Tybi of the coming year, the three hundred and eighty silver drachmas of the loan, without delay - - -

title: List of village guards
summary: The list consists of six names of persons who are apparently acting as police guards in the village of Senokomis.

title: Letter
summary: The contents of this letter must remain unknown, due to the fragmentary state of the papyrus.

title: Petition
summary: A petition of Paulus to Sarmates and Diogenes, known as riparii of the Oxyrhynchite nome for A.D. 365.

title: Labour-contract
summary: The papyrus is part of a labour-contract, put in the form of a homology.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 13 4 (English) - - - I have received by way of payment for the complete period nine golden solidi and a half - - - out of my wages for my food. And moreover, I acknowledge receipt of two golden solidi received already as earnest-money, and the remaining seven solidi and a half - - - - -, and that it is impossible for me to leave you before the end of this period without reason - - - - -, but if I should run away, I shall give you - - - golden solidi by way of fine - - - - - and that you, if you should want to dismiss me before the end of this period without due reasons, will be subject to the same fine. The agreement, drawn up in single copy (or: in duplicate), is valid and in answer to the formal question I have given my assent, giving as a pledge for everything that has been written above everything I have ans hall have - - - - - - by right of mortgage and as security - - - - -

title: Tax receipt
summary: A complete receipt for the payment of 9 gold carats, paid through Abba Georgius (the abbot of the monastery of Abba Sabinus).
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 72 (English) (Chi-Rho) Paid in the name of Johannes, son of Pillagios (?), through Abba Georgius of the holy monastery of Abba Sabinus, through Phoebammon the tenant, for the grain-tax of the village Aeos, of the assessment of the seventh indiction: nine gold carats, public standard, total gld. car. 9, public standard. (Chi-Rho) Flavius Collouthos, exceptor and pagarch of Antinoopolis, through me Gennadius assistent with God’s help, agrees with the receipt as stated above. Written on the 3rd of Payni of the 7th indiction.

title: Loan of money
summary: The papyrus is the upper part of a loan in the form of a χειρόγραφον.

title: List of expenses
summary: The papyrus contains a list of expenses in wheat for the 5th indiction of an unknown indiction cycle.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 79 (English) List of expenses of wheat for the 5th indiction: (For) Papnouthius wheat art. 18 (For) Josephius wheat art. 18 (For) Victor wheat art. 14 and for ⅓ solidus wheat art. 4 Through Psas wheat art. 18 Through Samouelius wheat art. 10 1/2 Through Cyriacus wheat art. 10 Through Adjoule for the rent wheat art. 3 ½ of - - - Through Johannes wheat 3 ½ Through Abramius wheat 3 ½ Through Mathias wheat art. 2 Through Johannes son of Apia Iai (?) wheat art. 2 Through the field guards of the village wheat art. 1 Through the - - - - wheat 1/3 For the rent of strings wheat art. 1 To the field guards wheat art. 1/3 (Verso) To the hunt for rent of bowstrings wheat 3 ½ Through Onophrius the assistant wheat art. 1/3

title: Private letter
summary: The papyrus contains a letter of Asclepiades to Demosthenes. Asclepiades offers his services and asks Demosthenes to buy things for him.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 31 (English) Asclepiades greets his dearest Demosthenes. Dius also called . . . .us, who brings you this letter, is a friend of mine. So help (?) him in order that (?) he may buy - - -. Write me back through him on any other matter you need in Ptolemais. And buy me a pair of plain cushions and receive the price from him - - -.

title: Document concerning appointment to a magistracy
summary: The text is concerned with appointment to a magistracy, but the type of document to which it belongs cannot be determined due to the fragmentary state.

title: Receipt of payment
summary: This papyrus is a complete Byzantine memorandum where Andronicus informs the deacons of a church that he has paid one gold carat on their account for Tybi.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 73 (English) (cross) (cross) To brother Phib and the other deacons - - - (cross) Andronicus has paid through me, Jacob, for the belated food-allowance, on the account of Tybi, xth, of the 8th indiction. I have signed.

title: Letter
summary: This papyrus is a letter from Maron to Ammon, concerning business matters.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 13 5 (English) Recto Maron greets his brother Ammon. You are advised to pay to A. . .syros the drachmas for three hundred and fifty lamps, each lamp at four drachmas - - - we have gone (?) there in connection with the bookkeeping (?) of your son and he correctly (?) keeps (?) it (?) - - - - - - - We have loaded for you, for Hates the Syenite, coarse ground barley, three loads and I shall add the rushes. He must make over the drachmas so that you may receive these things. I wish you health. Farewell. Verso Deliver it to Ammon, councilor, from Maron councilor of Oxyrhynchus.

title: Private letter
summary: The text on the recto is a private letter from Tryphon to his father. The verso might be the beginning of a sale contract of a slave, but this is not certain, due to further illegible lines.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 53 (English) Tryphon to his father, very many greetings. Be so kind, father, as to give Enas his sheet, for I have wronged Maron for it. Do not restrain him and make me come up. I have sent you the other --- through - - -

title: List of payments
summary: This papyrus contains a list of payments.

title: Declaration of land (?)
summary: This text might be a declaration of land, where the names of the emperors refer to an imperial edict ordering the registration of land.

title: List of workers
summary: The two fragments might contain a list of 'workers', made up 'at the order' of someone. The fragments may have been part of a longer list of perhaps several columns.

title: Private letter
summary: The papyrus contains part of a private letter containing greetings.

title: Christian letter
summary: This is a Christian letter, of which the content must remain unknown.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 64 (English) X to Y my sister: many greetings in the Lord. In the first place I pray for your well-being and your health to the Lord God. You did not write me about - - - our - - - Heraclides - - - you have received or not. And I have bought [what you needed?]. If you need other things write me about these as well. I wonder - - - until now.

title: Letter
summary: The content is obscure, although the meaning and the choice of the words seem to point to Christian precepts.

title: List of names
summary: The recto on this papyrus contains a list of names.

title: List of payments
summary: This papyrus contains a list of names and money payments probably of land taxes.

title: Official letter to the strategoi of the Arsinoite nome
summary: This fragment contains a copy of an official letter to the strategoi of the Arsinoite nome. The letter seems to be occasioned by problems with the availability information.

title: Three tax receipts
summary: This papyrus contains three receipts for a variety of money-taxes. The main tax concerned in these receipts is the λαογραφία or capitation-tax.

title: Chrysargyron account
summary: The papyrus is an account for payments for χρυσάργυρον tax, collected in a 15th indiction.

title: Agreement on oath and Draft of agreement
summary: The recto shows the fragmentary beginning of an agreement. The verso contains part of a draft of another ὁμολογία, with ὁ δεῖνα instead of a name.

title: Agreement on oath and Draft of agreement
summary: The recto shows the fragmentary beginning of an agreement. The verso contains part of a draft of another ὁμολογία, with ὁ δεῖνα instead of a name.

title: Contract of sale
summary: This papyrus contains part of a sale contract to a woman.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 50 (English) - - and (free) from guarding public dikes and from everything else whatsoever and from all state taxes and compulsory cultivation up to and including the past nineteenth year, because from the current twentieth year the products of the property belong to you the buyer at whose charge lie also the payments of every kind from the same twentieth year. The sale, written in x copies, is valid, and I shall also make it for you through a public office without receiving anything else from you, though the taxes and the scribal fees are at your charge. To the question posed by you whether this is done rightly and fairly I have given my consent.

title: Sale or cession of land
summary: This papyrus is probably about a sale or cession of land.
date_text: Late fourth or fifth century CE

title: Official documents (?)
summary: This fragment may belong to a roll of hypomnematismoi of a high magistrate acting as a judge. Otherwise, it may have been part of a petition in which reference is made to the ἀπόφασις of such a magistrate.

title: Contract of sale
summary: This papyrus contains a small fragment of a sale contract (or cession) of a house, courtyard and appurtenances.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 54 (English) [Aurelius A, son of B, function, of the illustrious and] most illustrious city of the Oxyrhynchites, [to Aurelia X, through her steward (Y son of?)] Artemidorus, from [- - -, acting without a guardian according to the Roman] customs on the basis of the ius liberorum, greetings. [I acknowledge having sold to you from now on forever - -] formerly belonging to Nilus [- - - now fallen to me as an ?] heir according to the laws [- - - house] and courtyard and appurtenances [- - - -] according to the [- - - of which the adjacent properties are: to the south: - - -] courtyard, to the west: [- - at the price on which we agreed for the aforementioned house and courtyard [and appurtenances being - - drachmas, which I received from you forthwith] in full [and in cash and in answer to the formal question I gave my consent. Therefore you shall possess and own with your descendants] and your successors [the house that was sold by me to you and the courtyard and] all the appurtenances and have power [over and use and control it as you wish, while there is no claim on it left whatsoever] for me or any other person on my behalf - -.

title: Account and list of names
summary: The text on the recto appears to be a tax-list concerning certain pieces of land in arouras with amounts in drachmas.

title: Account and list of names
summary: On the recto, an account appears to register expenses, perhaps for the military, including a calculation of some sort at the end. The text on the verso is a list of names.

title: Account (?)
summary: The recto has a dating formula. The verso appears to be a short account, which mentions amounts of artabas.

title: Petition to the prefect
summary: The papyrus contained a petition, with a subscription at the foot, sent to an official who was the superior of the epistrategos.

title: Loan of wine
summary: This papyrus is an agreement about a loan of wine.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 17 4 (English) Hermias, son of Hermias, Πέρσης τῆς ἐπιγονῆς, to Leon, son of Orsenouphis, greetings. I declare that I have received from you the three metretes of wine, including the half thereof, which I shall pay back to you in the month of Epeiph of the third year from my own vintage, new, good wine with the measure with which I usually pour out. If, however, I do not pay it back as is written, I shall pay you three thousand copper drachmas for each keramion as fine. This document shall be valid wherever it may be produced. In the third year, the ninth Pachon.

title: Declaration of a systates
summary: In this papyrus, Heraclius the συστάτης writes out a declaration regarding the so-called ἀναβολικόν tax.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 62 (English) To my lord and brother Alexander the monthly president and his associates of the guild of canvas and carpet workers from Heraclius the systates greetings. I have cancelled the name of Antinous, son of Heraclides, on account of the anabolikon tax of the thirteenth indiction, because I have no claim whatsoever on him on account of the said tax of his. I Heraclius the systates have signed.

title: Letter of Sulpicius Similis the prefect
summary: This papyrus contains a letter of Sulpicius Similis, who was prefect in Egypt in 107-112 CE. The letter is enmeshed in another letter, probably written by one of the addressees who has forwarded this copy after the receipt of the letter.

title: List of payments in kind for military annona
summary: This papyrus contains part of an account of payments of barley, meat and chaff destined for soldiers.

title: Account and draft
summary: The recto is an account, probably connected with taxing. The verso is probably a copy or a rough draft of a text concerning a citizen of Alexandria and a citizen of Oxyrhynchus.

title: List of names
summary: This papyrus contains abbreviated words, figures and fractions, probably of arouras. The text was no doubt drawn up for taxing purposes.

title: Tax receipt on the sale of a vineyard
summary: This papyrus contains the Greek tax receipt appended to a Demotic contract.

title: Private letter
summary: This papyrus is a letter about problems between various tax-collectors concerning the quantity of grain received by them, and its shipment. Also a landowner is involved and forced by threat of being poisoned to do what other desire from him.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 69 (English) Recto - - -] in order that also the part should be shipped by us instead of by you, and he said [- - - that the other(?)] tax-collectors shipped (the grain) among the first and if I need more grain in order that [- - -] and at the request he went down (to the ship?) and said that, what he wanted [- - - we shipped] among the first our grain, before the other tax-collectors [- - -] the landowner was drugged in order that he should accept more [- - -] sail on the tenth day to the estate and I hope that not [- - -] the month passed. [- - -] He showed us - - - Verso [- - -] - - - and if he had not communicated to me about - - - [- - -] - - - and I received the grain - - - more than the other tax-collectors - - - [- - -] - - - and in order that not be collected - - - [- - -] - - - the twentieth. However, from the first hour - - - [- - -] - - - there in order that - - - he spends time (?) for you on the grain - - -

title: Petition (?)
summary: This papyrus may be a petition.

title: Loan of money
summary: This document is a contract of loan for one gold solidus. The duration of the loan is unspecified.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 66 (English) Recto (1st hand) (cross) After the consulships of our masters Theodosius for the 12th time and Valentinianus for the 2nd time, the eternal Augusti (month and day). Aurelius Joseph, son of Timotheus, from the illustrious and most illustrious city of the Oxyrhynchites, to Aurelius - -, son of - - from the same city, greetings. I acknowledge having received from you on loan, from hand to hand out of your house, for my personal and pressing need, one single imperial approved solidus of gold, total 1 solidus of gold, as the capital sum, which, being free from all risk, I shall be bound to pay back to you whenever you require without - - - and without delay, you having the right of execution against me and against all my property. The contract, written in a single copy, is valid, and having been formally questioned I have assented. (2nd hand) I, Aurelius Joseph, son of Timotheus, the aforementioned, have received the one solidus of gold and I shall pay back whenever you require, as stated above. I - - -, have written on behalf of him as he is illiterate. (3rd hand) (Chi-Rho) By me, - - - Verso: Contract of Joseph

title: Account
summary: This papyrus seems to be an account of various expenses (?) for different persons.

title: Private letter
summary: The writers of this letter reply to an earlier letter by Hermias (?), who may be in some trouble, and they comfort him.

title: Letter
summary: This papyrus is a personal letter. The writer is the brother of the recipient.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 19 21 (French) Recto A Madame ma soeur très honoree, Nonna ton frère Isakios. Avant tout je prie Dieu tout-puissant que tu aies reçu ma salutation en bonne santé et bon état d’esprit avec ton mari et ta fille. Je voudrais que tu saches que j’ai reçu ta lettre par l’intermédiaire de Jean, le fils de Phiobammon et que j’ai lu ta salutation. Je te salue madame ma soeur et monsieur mon frère Petros et ta fille et tous ceux qui sont dans la maison par leur noms. Moi, la vieille, je te salue, madame ma fille et monsieur mon fils Petros et ta fille et tous ceux qui sont dans la maison par leurs noms. Moi, Hermes, je te salue madame et monsieur et tous ceux qui sont dans la maison par leurs noms. (Moi, Isakios), je te souhaite une bonne santé pour beaucoup d’années, madame ma soeur. Verso: Remets cette lettre à la ville d’Oxyrhynchus à madame ma soeur de la part de son frère.

title: Document relating to the magistracy of high priest
summary: This papyrus is concerned with appointments to municipal magistracies.

title: Contract
summary: This papyrus is possibly dealing with the (sale or) lease of a piece of property in or near the village Μνᾶχις in the Hermopolite nome.

title: List of nomes
summary: This papyrus contains a list of nomes in the genitive, each name followed by a numeral. On the back is a fragment of a private letter.

title: Account
summary: This papyrus seems to contain the ends of 6 lines of an account with numbers and a year, which could very well be the thirtieth year of Augustus.

title: Fragment of a document and account
summary: The recto mentions amounts (in artabas?). The verso contains the remains of two columns of a tax-list.

title: Draft including a prayer
summary: This papyrus contains a prayer for the intercession of the Virgin Mary and John the Baptist.

title: Contract
summary: This papyrus probably contains the end of a description of parties to a contract of debt.

title: Declaration on oath
summary: This papyrus contains the end of a document, followed by a declaration on oath by one of the parties concerned.

title: Draft (?) of a letter
summary: This papyrus is a draft (?) of a letter.

title: Receipt and account (?)
summary: On the verso of the papyrus are the remains of an account.

title: Official correspondence
summary: This papyrus contains an official correspondence of the court of law.

title: Two private letters
summary: This papyrus contains two private letters, the first from Heras to his/her sister Taphes, the second from an unnamed person to his/her sister Heras.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 25 42 (English) Recto Heras to Taphes my sister, many greetings. Avove all I pray you and your family are in good health. You should know that I have written to you twice and you have not written to me in answer to any of the letters. Receive from - - sandy-coloured sandals and a hair shirt and - - of a fruitcake and - - and dates - - - - - - - - - - - - - that you make the account (?) - - - - you will write to me. Goodbye To Heras my sister, very many greetings. Above all I pray you are in good health. I write you, until I come up, to thank you for the clothing. I do homage to the god on behalf of you. Verso To Philadelphia, give to my sister Heras.

title: Draft of a petition
summary: In this papyrus, the official addressed is asked to be severe in bringing 'them' to justice. The petition may have something to do with the office of prytanis.

title: Petition
summary: This papyrus contains someone who asks that the strategos (?) be instructed to settle the question in his favor.

title: Account and official letter
summary: This papyrus may be part of an official letter on tax-collectors.

title: Official document (?)
summary: This papyrus might have been official.

title: Report at the police of an armed robbery
summary: This papyrus contains a report of an armed robbery, reported to the ἀρχιφθλακίτης (the police).
translation_text: Buriks, ed.pr. (French) A Dicée, archiphylacite de Moithymis, de Pétosiris, fils de Thotis, fermier du roi à Moithymis. Le 25 du mois Pachon, à deux heures du soir, pendant que je me trouvais au temple, des hommes ont pénétré dans ma maison et dépouillé ma femme Thanous de sa robe, valant 200 drachmes, et Aynchis, sa mère, d’une robe valant 400 drachmes, et Thermoutarion, ma fille, d’une robe de toile valant 100 drachmes, en total 700 drachmes, les ont blessées de leurs couteaux de sorte qu’elles souffrirent des choses inouïes - - -

title: Bill of lading
summary: This papyrus contains a part of a bill of lading. The people mentioned must have been freedmen or veterans, as they all have Roman names, often with Greek cognomina.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 13 6 (English) Bill of lading of Gaius Iulius Probus - - - - of Marcus Arruntius Akas, field-guard - - - - of Herakleides, son of - - - - idetos, grandson of Akas, driver of a team - - - - of Tiberius Claudius - - - - of Tiberius Claudius, son of Kyknos, ten jars of sour wine - - - - likewise in the same name twenty sheets of papyrus of poor quality - - - - of Verrius Heraklas - - - x and 50 pambranches - - - - of Lucius Cartilius Mario, son of Pollio - - - - beds (?) - - - - likewise in the same name one centre-piece of a table - - - - of - - - -, son of Hygeinos, ten jars of sour wine - - - -

title: Report of sale of ground by landowner to the βιβλιοθήκη ἐγκτήσεων
summary: This papyrus contains a notification of a female landowner to the βιβλιοθήκη ἐγκτήσεων, mentioning the sale of her land.
translation_text: ed.pr. (French) (3me m.) Moi, Aurélius Horion, ai signé l’an 2 (ou 3) le 3 Phaophi. (1re m.) Aux Aurélius Hermaïskos, ancien agronome et Isidoros également appelé - - - - - -nos, bouleute, directeurs des archives d’immeubles du nome arsinoïte, de la part d’Aurélia Kasiané, aussi nommée Thermoutharion, fille d’Aurélius Deios, fils d’Hérakléidès, fils d’Ammonios du quartier de l’Hierakééon d’Apollonios, sous la tutelle de son père Aurélius Deios, nommé ci-dessus, du même quartier. Les 1¾ aroures, à moi appartenant, d’un héritage non divisé, dúne vigne en friche située près des villages de Kerkésoucha et de Phanèsis, du district d’Hérackleidès, terre catécique, des 2 (ou 3) aroures, partie des 7 aroures par moi déclarées, je déclare voulant les vendre au Aurélius Démétrios, fils de Ptolemaios, fils de Didymos, du quartier de l’Ision des Bithyniens, et d’Agathos Daimon, fils d’un père inconnu et d’Eudaimonis, affranchie d’Ant[onios?] etc. etc., à ces deux conjointement pour un prix de vente de 600 drachmes. (2me m.) Moi, Aurélia Kasiané, aussi nommée Thermoutharion, sous la tutelle de mon père Aurélius Deios, j’ai déclaré voulant les vendre les 1¾ aroures d’un vigne située près des villages Kerkésoucha et de Phanèsis, pour un prix de vente de 600 drachmes. Moi, Aurélios Deios, ai écrit pour ma fille qui ne sait pas écrire.

title: Loan of money
summary: A man from the village of Leukios in the third pagus borrows the sum of 6 talents and 3000 drachmas from a woman of Oxyrhynchus for a period of time between 19 and 49 days, probably a month.
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 13] Translation of A: To Aurelia Taïs alias Techosaria, daughter of Chosion from the eminent and most eminent town of the Oxyrhynchites, acting without guardian according to the ius liberorum, greetings from Aurelius Pagonis, son of Pagonis, his mother being Tathonis, from the village of Leukios in the third pagus. I declare to have received from you as a loan from hand to hand out of your house, six talents and three thousand drachmas in silver imperial coins, that is 6 talents and 3000 drachmas which I am bound to pay on the twentieth of the next month of Epeiph of the present 8th year/6th year, at the latest, without delay or excuse; if not, I shall pay you the interest that has been stipulated per mina per month for the time I shall be in arrears, while you have right of execution of me and all my goods. The chirograph - written in duplicate - is valid everywhere where it may be produced and for everybody who produces it on your behalf, and i answer to your formal question, I have given my assent. In the 8th year and the 6th year of our lords the Imperatores Caesares Constantinus and Licinius, the Augusti, during the consulate of Rufius Volusianus and Petronius Annianus the clarissimi, on the twentieth (?) of Payni. I Aurelius Pagonis, son of Pagonis have received as a loan the six talents and three thousand drachmas in silver, and I shall repay in the manner described above, at the appointed time, as is mentioned above and in answer to the formal question I have given my assent. I Aurelius Apollonius have written for him, because he cannot. Was written by me, Philonicus.

title: Official document
summary: The text on the verso appears to be an official document, perhaps concerning an inheritance. The recto is a hypothesis to Euripides of The Wise Melanippe.

title: List of the κωμογραμματεύς to the strategist about preparations for the visit of emperor Hadrian
summary: This papyrus contains a list of the κωμογραμματεύς to the strategist Asklepiades about the provisions for the visit of emperor Hadrian.
translation_text: ed.pr. p. 255 (English) To Asclepiades, strategus, from Horion, village scribe of Tholthis and Mouchinaryo in the lower toparchy. List of articles which at present, up to the twentieth of the present month of Choiak, have been stored in my district with a view to the visit of the most high Imperator Caesar Trajanus Hadrianus Augustus: barley 200 artabae, hay 3000 bundles, sucking-pigs 372 - - - 45, - - - 200, - - - 200, - - - 15, dates 55 artabae (?), fullgrow pigs - - - 8, - - - Egyptian sheep 200, - - - oil 3 metretae, chaff 7 baskets, unripe olives 3 artabae. I Horion, village-scribe, have delivered this list in the 14th year of Imperator Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, the 23rd of Choiak.

title: Acknowledgement of a debt concerning replacement of bad wine
summary: This papyrus contains an agreement in which is stated that the one party is bound, as the result of a judgement given, to supply to the other party 70 measures of wine of 5 sextarii.
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 17 10 (English) (1st hand)To --- the most worshipful, [from - - -, son of Herminus,] with my personal (?) guarantee, from the city of the Oxyrhynchites. I declare that I - - - owe to Your Worship and am bound to deliver seventy new sekomata of wine each 5 sextarii, say 70 sekomata of wine which I am bound to supply to you by virtue of a judgement given by - - - phronistes and by the presbyter of our - - -, in place of all the wine delivered by me to you during the wine pressing - - - of the first indicition and which was found - - - to be bad; and I agree to deliver the sais seventy measures of wine - - - at the vintage of the month Mesore in the current year 199 = 168, during the pressing of the wine of the with God’s will present indiction, without delay, at the risk of my own possessions. This document too, is valid, and is written in - - - fold, and in answer to the question I have given my assent. (2nd hand) I, Aurelius - - -, son of Herminus abovenamed, have now and owe the seventy new measures of wine and I shall deliver them at the vintage and I agree with all that is written above. I, - - -on, son of Petronius have, when aked to do so, written this for him since he is illiterate. Drawn up by me, Foibammon, Verso - - - from - - -, son of Herminus, the - - -

title: Notification of a theft of hay
summary: This papyrus contains a notification from the nauarchus to the riparii of the nomos of a theft of hay (or grass still in the fields).
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 13 8 (English) In the year after the consulate of our lord Theodosius, eternal Augustus, for the ninth time and of Flavius Constantius the most illustrious patricius for the third time on the 24th of Pharmouthi. To the Flavii Joseph and Theodoulos, riparii of the Oxyrhynchite nome from Flavius Theodoros, son of Theon, nauarchus of the same town. I do not understand how it has been possible, while in the present situation peace reigns everywhere – for fear of the power of our most eminent chief magistrate – that some evil-doers have invaded my land and stolen the hay - - -

title: Sale of land
summary: This papyrus is a fragment of a sale of land, including seven witnesses.

title: Letter (description)
summary: Obscure; perhaps Christian precepts embedded in a letter

title: Christian feveramulet
summary: This papyrus is an amulet, which should protect the wearer against fever.
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 19. 20] Le Christ ....... Le Christ est apparu. Le Christ a souffert. Le Christ est mort. Le Christ est ressuscité (des morts). Le Christ est enlevé (au ciel). Le Christ est roi. Le Christ sauve ... cus (?) qu'a mis au monde Gennaia, de toute fièvre brûtante et de toute sueur froide, quotidienne, journalière, déjà, déjà, vite, vite.

title: Military document
summary: The contents of this papyrus must remain obscure.

title: Military document
summary: The contents of this papyrus must remain obscure.

title: Act prescript
summary: This text is no doubt a contract, but its nature cannot be specified. The text begins with a full dating formula.

title: Mumientafel
summary: Name on a wooden tablet
translation_text: Tamoupis

title: Mumientafel
summary: A woman's name and age on a wooden tablet
translation_text: Senplenis, wife of Pata- -, - - 48 years old.

title: Wooden tag for the mummy of Tnaphersaïs
summary: A wooden tag for a mummy with order or permission for the burial
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 19:] Petosiris, son of Oteyris, says to Totoês, son of Imuthês, The Overseer of the Mystery of Osorbuchis: "perform burial for Tnaphersaïs, daughter of Hôros, her mother is Nb-t-wḏꜣ". Written in year 15, fourth month of the pr.t-season, day 6 (6 Pharmuthi). [Translation from Short Texts 2, including corrections:] Petosire, son of Uojhor, is the one who says to Tutu, son of Imhotep, the overseer of the mystery of Osobuchis: 'Perform burial for Tnafershai, daughter of Hor, her mother Nebuoj.' Written in year 15, 4th month of the šmw season (Mesore), day 6.

title: Wooden tag for a mummy
summary: A wooden tag for a mummy with order or permission for the burial
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 19. 37:] Year 20, firds month of the ꜣḫ.t-season, day 16 (= 16 Thoth). The scribe Teôs, son of Pamenôs the elder, the priest who enters, of the fourth phyle, says to Totoês, the Overseer of the Mystery of Osorbuchis: "perform burial for ..., son of Hatrês". Teôs, son of Pamenôs the elder, the priest who enters, of the temple of Ḥḳ, wrote (this). [Translation from Short Texts 2. 428 with correction:] Year 20, 1st month of the ꜣḫ.t-season (Thot), day 16. Scribe Jeho, son of Pamenh the elder, the priest who enters, of the 4th phyle, is the one who says to Tutu, the overseer of the mystery of Osorbuchis: 'Perform burial for Miumaht, son of Hatre.' In writing of Jeho, son of Pamenh the elder, the priest who enters, of the temple of Heka.

title: Wooden tag for the mummy of Hatrês
summary: A wooden tag for a mummy with an order or permission for the burial
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 19. 42:] Written in year 14, third month of the pr.t-season, day 16 (= 16 Phamenoth). The one who speaks to Totoês, son of Imuthês, the Overseer of the Mystery of Osorbuchis: "perform burial for Hatrês, son of Lamenthis", is the scribe Pelaias, son of Pamenôs the elder, the priest who enters, of the first phyle. [Translation from Short Texts 2. 426, with corrections:] Written in year 14, 3rd month of the šmw season (Epeiph), day 6. The one who says before Tutu, son of Imhotep, <the> overseer of the mystery of Osorbuchis, 'Perform buial for Hatre, son of Lamenth(is),' is scribe Pelaiha, son of Pamenh the elder, the priest who enters, of the first phyle.

title: Schooltablet with alphabetic acrostic on Prometheus' creation of mortals
summary: The tablet contains a mythological story: an elaborated version of the creation of mortals out of clay by Prometheus.
translation_text: (Translation from P. L. Bat. 25) Modelling, Prometheus made mortals from the divine image (?). According to the wish of the gods - - - (he made?) mortals by hand (?). So, having mixed earth and water, he made - - - . Hard to tear away and dark, he - - - finished the hair. - - - putting eyebrows in front. As a bridge in the middle of the eyes he stretched out and made the nose. - - - twice - - - and to the temples he fastened. He made within the mouth the treasury of the tongue for the speech. Adequately he formed cheeks, he combined - - - with cheeks (?)

title: Schoolbook of Paulus: writing exercise (?) and loan of money
summary: Only a few traces of writing are visible on side I A. Side I B: content unclear.                                                   Side II A preserves the text of a loan of hundred myriads of denarii. The writing on II B has almost completely disappeared.

title: Schooltablet
summary: Content unclear

title: Schoolbook of Aurelius Antonius: Isocrates, Ad Demonicum 1; Word Division and Mathematics

translation_text: [Translation from Short Texts 2. 1035, with correction:] (Greek text) Didymos the younger, son of Apuleius. He is sealed with (images of) hippocentaurodolphins. Year 5, Phamenoth (III pr.t) 24. (Demotic text) Hatre the younger.

translation_text: [Translation from Short Texts 2. 1036] (Greek) 1. [Ta]atriphis, daughter of Kolanthos, 2. the son of Lykos. 3. The sixth year, Epeiph 15. (Demotic) 4. Ta<t>riphy, the daughter of Kola<n>jo, the son of L[ykos], 5. the son of Tartary (?), - - -.

title: Anatomical instructions
summary: Anatomical information about intestines (the blind gut, the rectum and the sphinkter) given in questions and answers
genre: Papyri; Sub-literary (anatomy); Blind gut; Rectum; Sphinkter
translation_text: Recto: Why is it called blind gut? Because it does not have an opening, while all the others have an opening and are provided with passages. Why has the blind gut been created? Every ... is pleased at ... furnished by it, as you can understand according to the first argument on the gall bladder. Since, when there is something which is bilious, very pungent and betokening inflammation ... first ... the bladder / vessel, not less is there a danger to life in vomiting of bile, when what is contained is lessened, ... the gall bladder ... Verso: For what reason is it called straightened? Because it lies straight while the others ... revolving themselves in circles ... It is also called the rectum. Where is the closing muscle situated? In the final parts of the rectum. What is it like? With sinews and cartilage. For what purpose has it been created? For contraction ... rectum ... so that not easily ...

title: (Copy of) petition from priests asking the exemption from personally executing the work at the dikes
summary: Petition; Soknobraisispriests in Bakchias require their rights to be safeguarded and that they shall not be forced to execute personally the work on the dikes
provenance: Bakchias, Herakleidou meris, Arsinoite nome, province of Egypt
translation_text: To Flavius Apollonios, strategos of the meris of Herakleides of the Arsinoite nome, from Sisois, son of Orsenouphis, and Horos, son of Peteuris, and Peteuris, son of Peteuris, these three with the other priests of the famous temple of Soknobraisis in the village of Bacchias. A copy is appended of the petition which was brought to you with the endorsement of the archiereus. We ask that in accordance with the endorsement you order that our rights be safeguarded for us. The eighteenth year of Aurelius Antoninus and Commodus Caesars, the lords, the fourteenth of Mesore. The attached petition was appended below. And there follows the copy of the letter: Flavius Apollonios, the strategos of the meris of Herakleides of the Arsinoite nome, to his dearest Apollonios, basilikos grammateus of the same meris, greetings. Let the copy of the petition brought to me under seal with the endorsement of Ulpius Serenianus, the archiereus, in the name of the priests and pastophors of the village of Bacchias asking not to be forcecd to labor in person on the dikes be preserved. ... (2 1/2 lines) ... I, ...maios, the assistant, have received the petition .... thus: To Apollonios, basilikos grammateus of the meris of Herakleides of the Arsinoite nome, from Sisois, son of Orsenouphis, and Horos, son of Peteuris, and Peteuris, son of Peteuris, the three priests with the other priests of the god

summary: Recto: Flour and bread have been stolen from Aurelius Apollon, who, to the head of the police station, hands in a document concerning the theft; Verso: Record of expenses of textile business
date_text: Recto: 2nd half of the 3rd century A D; Verso: end of 3rd century A D
translation_text: Recto: To the head of the police station of the village ... from Aurelius Apollon, son of Ophellios, employed by Alypios, vir egregius and ducenarius. On the night before today, which is the 14th of the month Hathyr, some scoundrels, whom I do not know, came to the supply, which I have in the storehouse of the village of Narmouthis, and stole, from the storehouse, six artabas of wheat and white bread, which I had made for the honourable Artapates. Therefore I, by necessity, hand this in, making this known to you. Farewell! Apollon, 26 years old, whith a scar on my right shinbone. In the year...

title: Letter from Chrysaris to Leontios
summary: Private letter from Chrysaris to Leontios
translation_text: Recto: To my lord brother Leontios, Chrysaris, the daughter of Ambrosius, greeting. Be aware that I learned that you have been living in Narmouthis quite some time ago, and I did not want to come there to you because of the ejectment, even though I could also take with me Elias the baker who is my dependant and could collect from you. And many times the same Elias told me - I'm also going to get letters from the bishop or the vicarius to the councillors if they are going to collect from you.- And I did not want to for God's sake, lest you suffer ejectment. Please therefore send me the 2 solidi. If you want me to come there, write back to me, and I will come to you. If again you want to, send me the half amount in the meantime now, and after a few days the rest. I pray for your health for a long time, my lord. Verso: Deliver to Leontios the cumin seller from Chrysaris daughter of Ambrosios.

title: Eingabe an den praefectus Aegypti Q. Aemilianus Saturninus
summary: Petition to the praefectus Aegypti Q. Aemilianus Saturninus. Souchospriests in Bakchias complain about beeing forced to work at the dikes
provenance: Bakchias, Herakleidou meris, Arsinoite nome, province of Egypt
translation_text: An Quintus Aemilianus Saturninus, Präfekt von Ägypten, von Ammonios, Sohn des Horos, und Orsenouphis, Sohn des ..., und Peteuris, Sohn des Peteuris, aus dem Dorfe Bakchias im Herakleidesbezirk des Arsinoitischen Gaues. Es ist bei den Priestern des [grossen?] Gottes Souchos herkömmlich, die Kopfsteuer von acht Drachmen zu zahlen. Wir zahlen den Eintreibern des Dorfes, von denen wir auch Quittungen erhalten. Nichts anderes ... den Gottesdienst. Diejenigen, die ihre Lust daran haben, mächtiger Herr Statthalter, ... wir wollen ... der Kanäle ... ist selbst ins Haus hineingetreten und hat sie geheissen, an den Kanalarbeiten teilzunehmen ... wenn nicht, [müssten sie] 120 Drachmen [zahlen] ... durch Deine erhabene Entscheidung dem Gaustrategen auferlegt wird, die Vorhergenannten dazu zu zwingen, die 120 Drachmen, die sie uns wider die Vorschriften arbeitszwangsmässig auferlegt haben, zurückzuzahlen, bis dass wir unser Recht erhalten und damit wir durch Deine Tyche bei unserer eigentlichen Arbeit unbehelligt verharren können, ohne von irgendeinem zu arbeit herangezogen zu werden, da wir ja dem Fiskus die gewöhnliche Kopfsteuer von acht Drachmen zahlen, damit wir so Dein Wohlwollen erfahren. Lebe wohl! 2. Hand Ammonios und Orsenouphis und Peteuris haben die Bittschrift eingereicht; sie sind in eigener Person erschienen ... Im 7. Jahre, am 12. Athyr. 3. Hand Da kein Einspruch erhoben werden kann, dem Epistrategen einzureichen. 4. Hand Abzugeben.

title: Zauberpapyrus
summary: Magic formula on behalf of a little girl who is ill
translation_text: I, Abrasax, shall deliver. Abrasax am I! ABRASAX ABRASICHOOU, help little Sophia-Priskilla. Get hold of and do away with what comes to little Sophia-Priskilla, whether it is a shivering fit - get hold of it! Whether a phantom - get hold of it! Whether a daimon - get hold of it! I, Abrasax, shall deliver. Abrasax am I! ABRASAX ABRASICHOOU. Get hold, get hold of and do away with ... what comes to little Sophia-Priskilla on this very day, whether it is a shivering fit - do away with it! Wheather a daimon - do away with it!

provenance: Memnoneia, Thebes; or Hermonthis; province of Egypt
translation_text: (Tsiparis' translation) Psenmonthes Plenis, dem Sohn, einen Gruss. Komm zu uns und schaffe herbei den anderen Pastophoros, der imstande ist, die Festgaben zu verfertigen, und siehe den Phomonthes, den Sohn des Phthomonthes, Enkel des Hamsouphis.

title: Papyrus fragment
summary: Small fragment with slight traces of ink on the back side

title: Brief des Phaboullos an seinen Vater Theodoros
translation_text: Phaboullos an seinen Vater Thodoros die besten Grüsse. Grüss Monatiana, die Schwester, grüss Theodoros und Serapion, grüss auch Kapitoleina und Apolinaris und das Gebet für uns alle …

title: Privatbrief
summary: The writer of the letter wants the remaining amount of money for a wine delivery to be paid.
translation_text: (Tsiparis' translation) …rkos, dem Bruder und Herrn, einen Gruss. … Bruder, bemühe Dich um mich … , da Du stets Sorge um mich … dem Apollos und treibe ein … die Zahlung für die Weine vom kolophonischen Mass … unter welchen sechsundzwanzig milde, macht 26 … sechzig 6-Chus-Behälter, wovon ich 152 Drachmen erhalten habe … dem Apollos ..

title: Brief des Kasianes an seine Schwester Tikos
translation_text: (Tsiparis' translation) … Kasianes an seine Schwester Tikos viele Grüsse. Vor allem wünschen wir, dass Du guter Gesundheit bist. Wir sind gesund, wie auch unser Vieh, und beten für Dich bei den hiesigen Göttern. Gedenk an unser.. … und bete für uns bei der Herrin Leto.

title: Quittung über Getreidetransport

provenance: Euhemeria, Themistou meris, Arsinoite nome, province of Egypt

Stelle dem Iemouthes, dem Sohn des Imouthes, einen Esel, beladen mit Erve, und einen, beladen mit Linsen, am Thesauros des Kallistratos, des Sohnes des Pesskonouris, presbyteros?, zur Verfügung. Im 5. Jahre des Kaisers Tiberius Augustus, am 18. Mesore.

title: Staatsspeicher-Bescheinigung
summary: Receipt from official granary for three deliveries of grain
translation_text: (Tsiparis' translation) Zusammenfassung (der Zumessung an den) Staatsspeicher des Memnoneia-Bezirkes aus der Ernte des 20. Jahres für die Einnahmen desselben 20. Jahres der Aurelii Antoninus und Commodus, Caesaren, der Herren vom 16. bis (einschliesslich) 20. Pachon, im Namen des Psenmo.., des Sohnes des Pmenches, für den Memnoneia-Bezirk an Weizen 1/12 Artabe und im Namen des Tellon.., des Sohnes des Harpaes.., an Weizen 1/12 Artabe, vom 1. bis (einschliesslich) 5. Payni im Namen des Te.., des Sohnes des Tellonis, an Weizen 1/24 Artabe, macht insgesamt 1/6 + 1/24 Artabe,wie oben angegeben. Desgleichen vom 1. bis (einschliesslich) 5. Payni im Namen der Senphopho.., der Tochter des Harpaesimos, an Weizen 1/12 Artabe, und im Namen des Psenmo.., des Sohnes des Pmenches, an Weizen ein zwölftel, macht an Weizen 1/12 Artabe, insgesamt an Weizen 1/6 Artabe. Gezeichnet.

title: Kopfsteuerquittung
summary: The tax collectors Hierax and Poregebthis acknowledge, through the scribe Horos, the receipt of tax payments
genre: Ostraca; Receipt; Praktores argyrikon; Tax payment
translation_text: (Tsiparis' translation) Hierax und Poregebthis, Geldsteuereintreiber des Memnoneia-Bezirkes, durch Horos, dem Petechonsis, Sohn des Petechonsis, Enkel der Senma.. . Wir haben für Kopfsteuer und andere Merismoi des 21. Regierungsjahres zwölf Drachmen, im 21. Jahre des Hadrianus Caesar, unseres Herrn, am 17. Pachon erhalten. Im Jahre 22 am 9. Thoth durch Poregebthis 4 Drachmen.

title: Quittung über Getreidetransport

title: Quittung über Kopfsteuer(?)

Jahre des Kaiser Traianus … desgleichen am 13. Phamenoth … vier Drachmen am 25. Pharmouthi.

title: Quittung über Weizenlieferung

date_text: II A.D. (2nd half of) - III A.D. (1st half of)
translation_text: Im 11. Jahr, am 19. Thoth für Pentakomia (und) für das 10. Jahr im Namen der Senphtho.., Tochter des Pealesis, ein Achtel Weizen, macht 1/8 Artabe Weizen.

title: Quittung über Kopf- und Dammsteuer
summary: Through his secretaries a tax collector, Psansnos, has had poll-tax and tax concerning the dikes collected
genre: Ostraca; Tax receipt; Praktor argyrikon; Laographia; Dikes
translation_text: (Tsiparis' translation) Psansnos, Geldsteuereintreiber des Memnoneia-Bezirkes, vertreten durch Psen…, dem Psen.zisis, Sohn des Psenpanas. Ich habe für Kopfsteuer des 7. Jahres 4 Drachmen erhalten. Im 7. Jahr Hadrians des Herrn, am 9. Tybi. Am 4. Phamenoth 8 Drachmen. Desgleichen durch Ai…os, den Sekretär, 4 Drachmen. Für Dammsteuer des 4. Jahres 4 Drachmen. Desgleichen ……… 4 Drachmen. Desgleichen im 8. Jahr, am 6. Thoth für Kopfsteuer noch 3 Drachmen, zwei Obolen, zwei Chalkoi (einschliesslich des Zuschlages) entsprechend dem Normalsatz von 3 Drachmen und 4 Chalkoi.

title: Dammsteuerquittung

translation_text: (Tsiparis' translation) Für Dammsteuer des elften Regierungsjahres Domitians (hat) P..eie.., der Sohn des Patounthis, für den fünften Teil fünf Drachmen, zwei Obolen, macht 5 (Drachmen), 2 (Obolen) (bezahlt), am elften Pharmouthi weitere fünf Drachmen, zwei Obolen, macht 5 (Drachmen), 2 (Obolen). Im zwölften Regierungsjahr, am zehnten Hathyr.

I, Agathos Daimon, have signed.;And on Pauni 11, for the arithmesis of Pachon, Horos has paid twenty-eight dr., equal 28, and for Hieron, eighteen dr., equal 18.;And on Epeiph 10, for the arithmesis of Pauni, he has paid for Horos, son of Ptol(--), another thirty-six dr., equal 36.;And on Mesore [. ., for the arithmesis of Epeiph, Horos], son of Ptol(--)(?) has paid . . .;And in year 16, [- - -];has paid [- - -]

title: Account of Grain Charges
summary: Account (serving as a receipt?) of the public dues (and payments?) of a Marcus Titianus Val(--) for grain lands of various categories which he hold in Phil(adelphia?)
provenance: perhaps Philadelphia, Herakleidou meris, Arsinoite nome
translation_text: Marcus Titianus Val(--):;In Philadelphia(?), for sacred land, 4 1/8 artabai of wheat, (with) supplements of 17/24 (artabai), (equal) 4 5/6 artabai of wheat; for epibole, 1 7/8 artabai of wheat, (with supplements of) 7/24 (artabai of wheat), (equal) 2 1/6 artabai of wheat; for epibole of sacred land, 2 17/24 artabai of wheat, (with supplements of) 5/12 (artabai of wheat), (equal) 3 1/8 artabai of wheat.;And for Marcus, in Philadelphia(?), for arrears of year 6, likewise, for sacred land, 2 (artabai of wheat), (with supplements of) 1/3 (artabai of wheat), (equal) 2 1/3 artabai of wheat; for catoecic land, 1/12 artabe of wheat, (with supplements of) 1/24 (artabe of wheat), (equal) 1/8 artabe of wheat. Equal 12 7/12 artabai of wheat.

I have received from you eighteen artabai of barley, equal 18. Year 4 of our lord Probus Augustus, Mesore 26.

title: Drawing
summary: Pen drawing of Sobk, Souchos, the crocodile god of the Arsinoite nome, in the shape of his sacred animal, lying on a litter, which is placed on top of a large stone platform or altar. In front of the god (to the right of the drawing) a small altar with votive offerings. Souchos is reposing in his shrine, or under some sort of canopy, resting on four columns, with festoons and bunches of flowers(?) hanging from one to another. The roof is adorned by a profile cornice. To the left on the ostracon there is a large blot of ink with a dozen vertical lines mounting from it (lake with reeds?)

title: Receipt for money taxes
summary: Tax receipt for the annona and an associated charge called aphylismos, paid in money by Dioskoros son of Tiberinus
provenance: Karanis, Herakleidou meris, Arsinoite nome, province of Egypt
translation_text: In the 1st year, Pauni 3, has paid Dioskoros son of Tiberinus for the annona of 3 months, for Karanis, twenty-four drachmai, = 24 drachmai; and for the monthly cleaning out (aphylismos), for the account of Choiak, eight drachmai, = 8 drachmai; and, on Pauni 3, (for) the cleaning out of Pachon, forty drachmai, = 40 drachmai; and, on Epeiph 6, (for) the cleaning out of Pauni, eight drachmai, = 8 drachmai; and for the annona of Mesore, on the account of the cleaning out, sixteen drachmai, [=] 16 drachmai.

title: Private Letter
summary: Private letter concerning the use of oxen for the sowing
translation_text: Horion to Elias, my brother, gretings. Even if I was away, I learned the zeal that you had to furnish to us the oxen for the sowing. Please allow us, brother, to have them three more days, until we send them away (to you). Also, order to Eudaimon to go and take fodder from Dionysios - I have asked him to give it to me. I have confidence, brother, that you won't neglect me. I pray for your health, brother. Year 2, Tybi 18.

title: Delivery of grain from granary to harbor
summary: Delivery of 50 sacks (of wheat) is credited to the account of an unnamed village. The work of transporting this amount was spread over a period of almost exactly 6 1/2 months, from Mesore to Mecheir 15, i.e. July 25 - Feb. 9. If the ostracon records the total contribution of the village, it was a very small locality indeed. It is more likely that this short text is a summary memorandum of deliveries made by a single landholder also unamed. Cf. O.Mich. 9926, which covers deliveries made by Doulos, son of Aionis, over a period of more than three months.
date_text: Februar, 9 (10) of areganl year 5 in (late) IIIrd - (early) IVth A.D.
translation_text: Year 4 Mesore until year 5;Mecheir 15;of the village 50 sacks

title: Prayer to Dioscuri
summary: The prayer on the ostracon was addressed to the Dioscuri precisely in their role of Savior Gods. The text is unfortunately much damaged, but there is little doubt that it was a personal prayer. No other inference is quite reasonable in view of the name Muranos in l. 2. The meaning of the broken text in ll. 1-2 is in all likelihood accurately represented by "save Muranon". Nevertheless, the three-fold invocation has a liturgical flavor.
provenance: Karanis, Herakleidou meris, Arsinoite nome, province of Egypt
translation_text: Dioscuri Savors ...[;Muranon, Dioscuri [;Savors . [;Dioscuri [Savors

title: Loan of Money
translation_text: Aurelius Pemes, son of Patas and his mother Tanouphis, from the village of Karanis, about 45 years of age, with a scar on his right shin, to Aurelius Sarapammon, (formerly?) gymnasiarch, councillor, former strategus of the city of the Arsinoites, greetings. I acknowledge through the present document that I have received in loan from you in cash ten talents and three thousand drachmas in silver, in total 10 Tal., 3000 dr., for one month from the present day on, which I shall of necessity return on the 15th of the coming month Thoth, without interest forthwith. And if I fail to return them on the due date, I shall be subject to the (payment of) interest for the surpassing period and on demand you, the said Sarapammon, shall have the right of execution against both me, the acknowledging party Pemes, and against all my property, as if in consequence of a court decision. The document, written in a single copy, must be authoritative and in answer to the formal question I replied positively. In the consulship of our lord Constantinus Augustus (consul) for the 7th time and our lord Constantius, the most noble Caesar (consul) for the 1st time, Mesore 20. (M. 2) I Aurelius Pemes, have received the said ten talents and three thousand drachmas in silver and I shall return them on the due date in full as stated above. I, Aurelius Serenos, son of Heron, wrote for him, since he does not know letters. (Verso) Note of hand of Pemes from Karanis ...

title: Letter from a Churchman
translation_text: After leaving the town I shall go first to Takona and I shall travel up to the monastery of the Holy Georgios and there I shall take on a cargo. I looked for Mousaios, the child of Staurophania, because of the adaeratio I had provided (?) for him, and I found out to which place he had fled and look, I also forwarded to you his letter concerning the things he had sent me after he fled, in order that you too read it and (the letter) of our lord the scholasticus Bacchus. For you know well that I have his pledge that he will answer him. And look, I have written one letter to the same lord Bacchus in order that he comes to Takona and inspects his possessions. Please, ge hold not of the letter of Mousais and send it to me in order that get hold of it as testimony. (Verso) To my God-guarded lord and brother N.N., son of Phoibammon, ---, chartularius, Georgios, deacon, ---

title: Hyponema for the Nomarch Nikanor
summary: Four separate documents
date_text: III BCE: June 30, 237 or June 24, 212 or June 20, 195 BCE?
translation_text: (Verso) Memorandum to Nikanor, nomarch, from Heryothes. (M. 3) If it seems right to you, please, give an order to Achoapis concerning Petosiris, son of Thotortaios, a farmer from Philadelphia, in order that he be released (from jail) after a surety has been provided and that he will be there for the collecting of the wheat and --- on the threshing floors. He cultivates 55 arouras of land. (M. 4) Nikanor to Achoapis, greeting. After having checked whether he is not subject to a rather serious accusation and after having given a surety that he will stay, release him from jail in order that he will be present at the collecting of wheat. Farewell. (Recto) --- to Achoapis ---. (M. 5) To Alexandros. Achaopis to Alexander, greeting. I have sent you the letter from Nicanor. Act now according ---. Year 10, Pachon 13. ---.

title: Contract for a Parachoresis
genre: Papyri; Documentary; Contract; Land cession; Parachoresis
translation_text: Year fourteen of Nero Claudius Caesar August Germanicus Imperator, on Pachon the tenth, in the city of Oxyrhynchoi. N.N. daughter of N.N. son of Kephalas and of Tausarapis daughter of Horus, with as her guardian her husband (?) N.N. son of N.N. from --, acknowledges to Berenice, daughter of N.N. son of Dionysios and of Berenice daughter of Dio--, from ---, that she has ceded to her, in accordance with the administrative arrangements of those controlling cavalry affairs and on the basis of a memorandum submitted by her to the official in charge of the registry of military settlers' land grants, three arouras of catoicic land and land bought for conversion into catoicic land, belonging to her near Nesla in the Upper Toparchy in the kleros of N.N., together with the yearly rents thereof, of which the neighbors are: to the South --, to the North --, to the West --, to the East --, in order that the three arouras shall belong to Berenice and her offspring along with the other rights and benefits, irrefutably for all time, in accordance with the ordinances and instructions, and the she, the acknowledging party, has received from Berenice the agreed upon among of cession money, i.e. n silver drachmas of imperial money, on the spot and in full, at the moments when this was urgent, and that regarding this cession N.N. will not violate the contract and will not attempt any fraud, in any way whatsoever, and that the same (woman) will absolutely hand over to Berenice or to her representatives the three arouras fully guaranteed and free from all the public imposts and other dues, from previous years up to the 5th of the Epogomenai of Mesore of the past thirteenth year and including the thirteenth year of Nero Claudius Caesar August Germanicus Imperator. If she violates any of these provisions in the contract her action will be invalid and she shall forfeit to Berenice or to her representatives for every specific breach both the damages and a fine of n silver drachmas and to the state the sum, and nevetheless the contract as drawn up in accordance with their mutual harmonious agreement, shall be binding. (M. 2) In the 14th year of Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus Imperator, on Pachon the tenth. It has been registered through N.N., tax-farmer (representing his colleagues). (Translation of P.NYU II 15a)

translation_text: Phallas, son of Hatres and mother N.N., from the city of Oxyrhynchus, acknowledge that he has sold to Herakleides, son of Didymus, grandson of/alias (?) N.N., his mother is Helena, from Aphrodison in the Small Oasis a female, white, full-grown donkey without a brand, for an agreed price of three hundred twenty drachmas of minuted imperial silver coinage which acknowledging party, Pallas, son of Hatres, has received on the spot in full from the said Herakleides and (Pallas acknowledges) that he will guarantee (the donkey). And if somebody lays his hands on it saying he is the owner, the acknowledging party will remove him at his own expenses or - if he does not - he will hand back to the said Herakleides the afore-mentioned price which has been paid to him. The (contract of) sale is authoritative. Year 7 of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesares the lords Augusti on the 2nd of the month Hadrianus. I, Pallas, son of Hatres, have sold the said donkey and I have received the said three hundred twenty drachmas of silver as stated above. I, Paesis, son of Ploutarchos, from the city of Oxyrhynchus, have written for him as he does not know letters.
