summary: A tax receipt (100 nomismata) for the first levy of the chrysika of the first indiction, delivered to the magistrates of Aphrodito, and signed by Heliodorus, treasurer of the eparchy of the Thebaid. The cursive is competent and neat, and similar to that of 67033. Signature of the same hand as the signature of 67033. Illegible subscription on the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Documentary papyrus.
summary: More than one(?) document, very abraded.

title: Tax receipt
summary: A tax receipt (114 nomismata 14 keratia) signed by Heliodorus, general treasurer of the eparchy of the Thebaid, for the third levy of the chrysika of the sixth indiction. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Tax receipt
summary: A tax receipt (114 nomismata, 8 keratia) for the third levy of taxes of the 4th indiction, signed by Victor, treasurer general of the eparchy of the Thebaid. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt issued to the demosios logos of Aphrodito by the tribune of Antaiopolis, on the receipt of  his annual salary. Subscription almost completely faded on the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Tax receipt
summary: Tax receipt (36 nomismata) for the kanonika of the 8th indiction, issued to the magistrates of Aphrodito by the treasurers Ammonios and Victor. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a receipt
summary: Fragment of receipt issued to the magistrates of Aphrodito, attesting that they have paid the tax in kind of the 7th indiction, through the intermediary of the hypodectes Abraham. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Tax receipt
summary: Tax receipt, signed by Fl. Origenes, and issued to the protocometes of Aphrodito, for having paid 8 nomismata less 31 keratia 1/2. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt issued to Apollos son of Dioscoros by the pagarchs of Antaiopolis for the astika of the 3rd katabole  of the 8th indiction. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a receipt
summary: Fragment of receipt relative to the annona militaris, issued to the magistrates of Aphrodito by Fl. Psates, actuarius of the numerus of Antaiopolis. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Order
summary: Order addressed to Ioannes, hypodectes of Aphrodito, to pay to the soldier Hadrian 2 nomismata less 2 keratia, debited from the receipts of the 2nd indiction. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Account (?)
summary: On the recto, fragment of a  financial roll divided into two columns. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Divorce
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Large hand, approximating a bookhand; below 7 lines in a cursive hand. On the recto one illegible short line.

title: Poetry
summary: The verso has been used by a poet of Aphrodito, who wrote there a short piece of poetry in honour of Ioannes, dux of Thebaid (c.AD 553). Unfortunately the verso has been badly damaged, the surface of the papyrus is frequently used and the ink, already very faint, has, in many places, become invisible. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros. See Fournet, Hell�nisme (MIFAO 115) p. 416-417 no. IV 20.

title: Fragment of an account
summary: Fragment of a financial roll issued by the office of Antaiopolis(?). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Account
summary: Account issued by the Antaiopolis office of finance. The expenses are assigned to four classes: annonae and synetheiai (salaries of the officials); kanonika (general taxes of the empire); and special taxes for pagarchy. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros

title: Account
summary: Account similar to P.Cair.Masp.057, but relating to expenses of Aphrodito. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.
date_text: VI Century AD (Cols 1-6: AD 534-35; Cols 7-8: c.AD 520-30)

title: List of contributions
summary: List of contributions paid by inhabitants of Aphrodito, drawn up by a certain Dioscoros, undoubtedly the protocometes of this name. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter
summary: Letter addressed to Dioscorus and  to Apollos, protocometes of Aphrodito, by a certain Menas - perhaps the pagarch of Antaiou, cited in 67002.  Menas complains of the lateness of the village of Aphrodito in the payment of taxes. Interestingly, he invites Dioscoros to come and see him, to talk about the affairs of the kome. Address on the verso - very crude, one line. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter
summary: Letter addressed to Apollos, protocometes of Aphrodito. The sender charges him with watching over the affairs of the convent of [�] , and with collecting the sum of 2 nomismata due by one of the peasants of Ammonios the comes, to use it as indicated. The document is complete, but is made up of three separately discovered fragments. Address on verso; the edge of the papyrus having disappeared, except on the right, nothing remains but a few words. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.
date_text: First half of VIth Century AD

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: Fragment of a letter addressed to Apollos, father of Dioscoros. Address in a single line on the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter
summary: Letter addressed by the notary(?) Anastasios to Dioscoros, on the subject of an act of  antimisthosis which the clerks of the church of St Mary have agreed with a certain Paos, and which Antastasios witnesses. Well preserved address, containing a correction. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter
summary: Letter addressed to Dioscoros, protocometes of Aphrodito, announcing to him that a soldier, wrongfully lodged in one of his properties, has been ordered to evacuate it (c.f. Cod. Just. 12. 41.). The address on the verso of the document contain the name and position, or administrative title, of the addressee. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract with a wet nurse
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. The same hand as BGU IV 1099. Duplicate of this document: inv. nr. 250.

title: Letter
summary: Letter addressed to Dioscoros, protocometes, from Phoibammon, one of his clients. The bearer of the letter will hand over two keratia  to Dioscoros, so that he can pay a debt. There also seem to be other obscure arrangements. The address on the verso of the document state both the name and position of the addressee and the name of the sender, and occupies two lines. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter
summary: Letter addressed to Dioscoros, son of Apollos, by an agent sent to Constantinople about a lawsuit apparently taken to the tribunal of the prefect. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter
summary: Letter addressed to Apollos or to his son, demanding a remittance of money, because the  boethoi  have refused to issue the tax receipt as long as the extra 7 keratia have not been paid. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: Fragment of a letter. The correspondents' names are lost. The sender tells of the accident suffered by a third person - also unknown - and gives news of the recipient's children. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Deposition
summary: Written deposition - ekmartyrion - by Kollouthos, one of the employees of the defensor of  Antaiopolis in favour of  Dioscoros of Aphrodito, against a son of Mousiaos (name lost), who has damaged Dioscoros' fields. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Deposition
summary: Written deposition - martyria - of a witness in favour of a woman named Stephanous whose husband Psates, 'reader' of one of the churches of  Aphrodito, has alienated a dower. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Declaration
summary: Written declaration, in which an unknown person certifies that a nun named Eulogia, who lives in his house, is neither a slave nor of servile family. The document starts on the verso and continues on the recto.

title: Letter concerning a safe - conduct
summary: Safe-conduct - logos - issued by the commentariensis to three protocometes of Aphrodito and to the boethos,  who probably were summoned by him. The brothers Enoch and Phoibammon are perhaps the same who were, successively, the bursars / stewards of the convent of Aphrodito (cf. 67096, l.6 et 16). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Two fragments of petition
summary: Two fragments of a petition (libelloi) addressed to Fl. Victor, riparios of the village  of Aphrodito, by the farm worker Aurelius Victor. Only the beginning and the end remain. The middle, which contained the explanation of the matter, has disappeared. Victor, son of Besios, who is here accused, is perhaps the same who wrote 67091. In this case he would, in the interval, have left the service. Subscription on the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Deed of surety
summary: Deed of surety - eggue. Four inhabitants of  Aphrodito answer to the pagarch Julian of Antaiopolis for the person of a monk called Enoch. If they are unable, the guarantors will pay six  nomismata. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Documentary papyrus.
summary: Badly preserved document

title: Deed of surety
summary: Deed of surety - anadoche.  Aurelius Psaios, a farm worker, undertakes to give to his employer Dioscoros, son of Apollos, a part of his salary from the 12th indiction to come, to stand surety for his brother in law Apollos. Damaged subscription on the verso of the document. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Donation
summary: Donation inter vivos of a building not yet constructed, and of a sum of two nomismata. The monk Psates, the donor, lives in the diakonia of the convent of Apa Apollos: the founder of this convent is Apollos, son of Dioscoros, and father of Dioscoros, the protocometes of Aphrodito. We should not think, despite the closeness of the places, of the monastery of Apa Apollos at Baouit: the founder of this probably lived in the 4th century and was, according to the Synaxary, born at Panopolis to a father called Amani (Ammonios?). Psaios has already received, in return for a security paid into the hands of Phoibammon, previously the steward of the convent, the right to have at his disposal a building - kellion - situated on the grounds of the diakonia. He now intends to enlarge it, or to build a new one next to it, to found a xenodocheion, a hospice, destined to receive foreign monks who arrive at the convent. Permission was granted him after a few difficulties, and he undertakes to bequeath this new building to the community while restoring the old one, and not to ask for the security, paid earlier, back. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract
summary: Contract of sale for a piece of land, agreed between Hermauos, seller, and Isaac son of George, buyer. The contract is incomplete. The beginning and the end have been cut off by the person who has used the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Poetry
summary: On the verso of 67097, the poet of Aphrodito has written some small poems in dactylic hexameters and iambic triameters, as well as a long piece of prose - a father's sentence of disinheritance, against his daughter. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Sale of land
summary: Sale of land. All of the proper names of the individuals involved have disappeared. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Rental agreement
summary: Rental, for one year, of a palm grove belonging to Aurelius Apa Victor, by someone named Aurelius Promauos. The rent will consist of  12 artabes of dates. The subscription on the verso of the document is incomplete, but the sign for misth�sis is preserved. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Rental agreement
summary: An agreement relating to the rental of a piece of land.  Aurelius Anouphios, of Aphrodito, rents for one year a piece of land belonging to a church of  Antinoe. The subscription on the verso of the document is incomplete, but the sign for misthosis is preserved. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Two fragments of a contract
summary: The demosios logos of the village of Aphrodito rents out some land to a person whose name is lost. The two remaining fragments provide us with the beginning and the end of the contract, but their middle, where the transaction was set out, is lost. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Rental agreement (land)
summary: Aurelius Ioannes rents a vineyard belonging to [...], daughter of Ioannes the comes, for a period of ten years, in return for 126 aggeia of wine per aroura of land. Each  aggeion is valued at seven xestes, and the farmer is obliged to provide the clay jars himself. He rents, at the same time, a palm grove, lands whose nature is not specified (ktemata: most likely, cereal fields) and a piece of uncultivated land, which he undertakes to transform into a vineyard. All this on the same conditions as an individual named Ioannes, the previous tenant of the same properties. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Rental agreement (land)
summary: The deed is drawn up in the form of a cheirographon, addressed by the proprietor to the tenant. The priest Ioannes, son of Ioannes, represented by Senouthos, son of Apollos, allows Aur. Besarion the use of a piece of land located at  Aphrodito for two years. The dues will consist of corn, cheese and honey. In three fragments. Subscription on the verso, containing the names of the two parties and identifying the deed as antimisthosis. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Testament of Pachnoubis
summary: Pachnoubis leaves practically all his posessions to his wife Tathotes, while only leaving a mattress and a bed to his to sons of another mother. At the end the signatures of the testator and six witnesses. The document is a copy, written in a clear, semi-cursive hand.

title: Lease of a garden
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Rather careful script. The top is abraded. The document consisted of two sheets stuck together, of which the right one is lost (approximately 35 letters per line are missing). On the verso of the left sheet are two loan contracts, in a different hand from that of the recto (BGU IV 1150).

title: Rental agreement (land)
summary: Aurelius Psempnouthios, of the village of Aphrodito, rents out  a piece of land belonging to the heirs of  Apollos, son of Dioscoros, in return for a rent of one artaba of corn per year. The contract shall be valid as long as it is acceptable to the proprietors. Complete, in two fragments. The subscription on the verso is in two lines. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Rental of a potter's workshop
summary: The potter Aurelius Psais takes out a lease, for as long as he lives, on the third part of a potter's workshop belonging to the heirs of the two sisters Helene and Maria, at Aphrodito, in return for a rent of 2400  jars (koupha). The verso contains a subscription in three lines, which preserves the sign for misthosis. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a rental agreement (land)
summary: Fragment dated the 3rd year of Maurikios. Very badly damaged. All the lower half is missing, and the left part of the top half. The remaining quarter is rather damaged, and has no interest except  the date it carries. At the time of publication, this was the only papyrus from Aphrodito which referred to the reign of Maurikios. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Rental of land
summary: Rent of a piece of pasture land belonging to Apollos, son of Dioscoros, by the shepherd Aurelius Phoibammon. The contract is not dated, a few lines of the top must be missing. The right half is missing for almost the entire length. It looks as if the document was thrown in fire. Seven fragments. The subscription is a single line. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Rental of land
summary: Document relating to the rental of land. Three fragments. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Agreement
summary: Agreement between Dioscoros, son of Apollos, and Victor the weaver. Victor has rented to Dioscoros an aroura of land to sow flax. Moreover, the seeds and the water necessary to irrigate the field are provided by the proprietor. Victor undertakes to pay, for everything, a fee of 1 nomisma less 2 keratia at the time of the third - and last - tax levy of the 12th indiction. The farm rent will thus be paid one year in advance, since the sowing will not happen until during the 13th indiction - cf. below, 67128-67129). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Transfer of taxation
summary: Agreement between Dioscoros, son of Apollos, and Victor the weaver. Victor has rented to Dioscoros an aroura of land to sow flax. Moreover, the seeds and the water necessary to irrigate the field are provided by the proprietor. Victor undertakes to pay, for everything, a fee of 1 nomisma less 2 keratia at the time of the third - and last - tax levy of the 12th indiction. The farm rent will thus be paid one year in advance, since the sowing will not happen until during the 13th indiction - cf. below, 67128-67129). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Transfer of taxation
summary: Document similar to P.Cair.Masp. I.67117. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Sale of two slaves
summary: Eulogia and her daughter Rhodous have been sold to a certain Ioannes, a financial employee (trakteut�s). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros. Crumbling, and very incomplete. This papyrus was found crumpled inside another roll, being used as stuffing, hence its very fragmentary state. In addition, various fragments (especially signatures) were found in the same roll, very damaged; the first three may form part of the same document.

title: Undetermined contract and poetry
summary: On the verso, the last lines of another(?) contract, followed by several pieces of verse in hexameters and in iambic triameters which form a small body of encomia on the relatives of the dux Callinicus.  Written by Dioscoros, son of Apollos, this group includes birthday poems on Colluthus (an administrator at Antinoe, and a kinsman of Callinicus), comes Dorotheos, and an unnamed recipient. The group also includes a birthday poem on Constantine. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Sale of land
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. The hand is like BGU IV 1121. The back is blank.

title: Borrowing contract
summary: Borrowing contract agreed between Aur. Apollos, son of Dioscoros, protocometes of Aphrodito, and Aurelius Anouphios. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Borrowing contract
summary: Fl. Victor, priest of the Church of Aphrodito, and Fl. Apollos, both originating from the village of Aphrodito, but now residing at Constantinople, acknowledge the receipt from the banker Anastasios of the sum of 20 gold coins, which they undertake to return to him after a delay of four months, with the legal interest (8%) in the branch of his bank in Alexandria. In several pieces. The fragments of the beginning are very mutilated: they were found far from the remainder, from which they differ by colour as much as by state of conservation. Nevertheless the connection of lines 31 and 32 is certain. The left margin is very irregularly torn. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Acknowledgement of a debt
summary: Aurelius Apollos, son of  Hermauos, acknowledges that he owes  Fl. Dioscoros, son of Apollos, for a purchase of wool, one third of a gold coin, which he will return to him the first day of epagomenai. The debtor offers, as surety, all he owns. On the verso, a subscription whose beginning is entirely faded, and of which we are able to distinguish only the end. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Acknowledgement of a debt (?)
summary: This deed preserves several specific terms: asphaleia (12), suggraph� (36), opheil� (38): but it is quite difficult to determine its precise nature. It seems to be a land-rental agreement disguised under the form of borrowing. Psais, son of Besios and of Tasais, deacon of Aphrodito, has rented from Dioscoros, protocometes of the same village, a piece of land of one aroura, for which he will pay the rent (phoros) in money. Moreover the proprietor has supplied the seeds - flax - and it seems also that the irrigation of the land is guaranteed by him - by means of his 'saqiah', situated in a nearby field. Psais undertakes to pay off as his rent-dues, and as the price of the seed and the irrigation, the sum of 1 nomisma less 2 keratia. Finally, the tenant will not take effective possession of the land until the time of sowing of the 12th indiction, which will not prevent him paying the rent at the end of the 11th, consequently one year before the harvesting. A similar transaction, although drawn up in a different form, appears in 67116. The subscription on the verso is in two lines. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract
summary: Contract similar P.Cair.Masp. 128, agreed between the same Psais son of Besios, now a priest, and the same Dioscoros, son of Apollos. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Acknowledgement of a debt
summary: Mousaios, son of Callinikos, deacon, acknowledges having borrowed from Dioscoros, son of Apollos. The end is missing. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Official minutes of a juridicial act agreed before the praeses of Thebaid
summary: Official minutes of a juridicial act agreed before the praeses of Thebaid: Theodosius, courier of the officium, grants discharge of a debt of 15 golden coins 1/3 to the heirs of a certain Isidorus, who had performed the same function, notably to a woman named Theus - the widow of Isidorus. The payment is effected by Psoios, numerarius of the same officium, who had stood as guarantor for Isodorus at the time of the borrowing. It is still possible that that this numerarius had simply enough to extinguish the debt on the sum that he was given responsibility for paying to Isidorus, and which he had died without having received. Theodosius, finally, withdraws all rights to the possession of a house which had belonged to the deceased, and which had, it seems, been mortgaged in guarantee of the debt. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Piece of iambic verse
summary: On the verso, a piece of iambic verse - an encomium on Victor the prefect - written by Dioscoros, following five lines of tachygraphy. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a receipt
summary: Fragment of a receipt issued by Annarios (?), stationarius. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a receipt
summary: Fragment of receipt issued by Victor, in the name of the sons and heirs of the decurion Kyros, to the heirs of Apollos, represented by two of their number, Dioscoros et Phoibammon. The left part is almost completely missing, and the last line is completel

title: Beginning of a contract (contents lost)
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Small, skillful and clear hand. On the recto a loan contract by a different hand but of the same date (BGU IV 1147).

title: Fragment of a receipt
summary: Fragment of receipt issued by Kyros, decurion of Antaiopolis to Apollos son of Dioscoros, hypodectes. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragments of a receipt
summary: Two fragments of a receipt  for 50 nomismata, issued by Stephanos, actuarius of the detachment of Antaiopolis, to the protocometes of Aphrodito. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Account of the expenses of comes Ammonios
summary: From a book of six leaves. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros. (A) Account of the sums remitted to the hypodektes, on the receipts of Menas, for the 5th indiction. There follows the listing of the debtors and of the duties they have settled, sometimes with the indication of the use which has been made of the payment. On the verso, account of the sums, 6th indiction, after Ammonios' return from Antinoe; Paophi. (B) Account of the dues in kind of the properties of the illustrious comes, 8th indiction. The first has been delivered to Apa Isaac, of the deanery. These taxes are the chrysika (paid in cash), as opposed to the embole or payment in kind. On the verso, account of public taxes paid by the comes Ammonios, 8th indiction. (C) Account of the payments in kind of the 9th indiction. On the verso, account of taxes in cash due for the properties of the illustrious comes. One part of the demosia of the 9th indiction has already been counted above; but they were only those of the first levy, and furthermore the properties located at Aphrodito do not figure in the picture. The rest of these taxes are placed here. (D) This bears only the copy of an entagion for the annona, without doubt of the 9th indiction.

title: Account relating to the affairs of comes Ammonios
summary: From a book of six leaves. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros. (A) Verso - Account of sums collected in money on the domains of the illustrious comes, 8th indiction. There follows a list of settlers, with the sum paid by each of them. (B) Account of the price of barley belonging to the comes, 7th indiction; this is what has been sold by the date due. It concerns taxes paid to comes Ammonios for the 7th indiction, and sold or given over by him to different officials. Verso - Account of the annona of the illustrious comes Ammonios, for the exercise of the 7th indiction - cf. 67138. List of colonists, indicating the quantity of corn tendered to the bursar by each of them. (C) Account of corn for the convent of Psinabla, 7th indiction, in the hands of Apa Phib, monk and bursar. The tax due by Ammonios to the convent of Psinabla is thus only of 100 artabas, and the contribution of three settlers is enough to make it up. Verso - Account of the quantities of corn delivered for the deacons of the convent of Aphrodito, in the hands of the very reverend Apa Isakios: among the taxes in kind the properties of the illustrious comes Ammonios 7th indiction. (D) Account of taxes in kind of the properties of the count: 7th indiction. It is a resum� of the preceding pages; although the figures have disappeared almost everywhere, they have been restored by the editor. The expenses amount to 913 artabas of corn. Verso - Account of barley from the properties of the illustrious comes, 7th indiction. The list is partially double; to the left, the names of the peasants who have provided the barley: To the right, the names of the buyers. (E) Account of the expenses incurred for the business of the comes in work for the 5th indiction, to be charged on the revenues in kind of the 6th indiction. The word ekphoria indicates that the expenses will be balanced by the equivalents in kind(?). In this paragraph we see appearing the kometika and the astika taxes. Verso - Account of the collection of revenues in money of the comes for the fiscal year of the 6th indiction. (F) Account of the hypodektes Apollos, 15 Phamenoth: he has received 2 nomismata less 3 keratia 3/4 plus 2 trimesia less 1 keration, plus 1 trimesion. Verso - Account of Menas. In the course of the 5th indiction for the 6th indiction, Artemidoros, singularis, for the barley of the monastery of Apa Sourous provided, for the 6th indiction: 2 nomismata less 4 keratia. Next comes a similar account for the 6th indiction. Finally, at the bottom of the page, a list of the peasants having paid their contributions in barley, for the 8th indiction.

title: Private Account
summary: A private account, relating to the flocks of the comes Ammonios. The lands, here as in 67057 (col III),  are divided into categories according to cultivation: worked fields, gardens, vineyards, marshland, date forests.

title: Accounts relating to the administration of private land
summary: The manuscript comprises seven mutilated leaves, of which none seem to have preserved the original height; in addition to the many lacunae, the surface of the papyrus is in places so worn that parts of the document are almost entirely illegible. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros. (A) The recto is almost completely illegible. Verso - Account of expenses, from Mesore to Hathyr. (B) Similar accounts to those appearing in the previous sheet. At the bottom of the page another hand has written a recipe for the treatment of migraine. Verso - Badly damaged, this page remains of doubtful meaning, at least at the beginning. The words h�mer(ai) g, b, a which seem to relate to certain names perhaps represent the number of days of work provided by each workman. (C) This concerns flocks, one under the supervision of Sister (Pelias?), the other under the supervision of Dioscoros; the animals - apparently sheep - are divided up in different classes, following extremely ancient usages. Verso - Illegible. (D) It contains accounts similar to the previous page. Verso - Illegible. (E) Accounts following those set out in A verso. Here, we go from the end of the month of Mecheir up to the beginning of Epeiph. Thus, although in very bad condition, this seems to represent a complete set of accounts for the year. Verso - Very badly damaged. It is an account of wool gathered. (F) The recto is illegible. Verso - Account of a flock of goats, in the care of the farmer Apollos. (G) The recto is illegible. Verso - Account of shearing of the sheep, as in E verso.

title: Account of receipts
summary: Account of receipts, drawn up by a certain Senouthes - a merchant (?) - for the 11th indiction. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Three private accounts
summary: Three private accounts, in the same hand.  The author sets out a list of people who have stolen from him a part of his property, as well as his animals. The recto bears the two first accounts, separated by a large empty space. The verso bears a third,  written at a right angle in relation to the verso, which proves that the papyrus is not a detached leaf from an account book. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Private Account
summary: Account of the same kind and with the same handwriting as 67143. The papyrus is incomplete: it lacks a strip of around 3 centimetres on the left edge. The verso has not been used. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Accounts
summary: Accounts of a distributor (actuarius?) of the annona militaris, Isaac Mauros. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Payment of an eranos-loan
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Small hand, at times difficult to read; the lower part of the text is badly preserved. At the foot of the page and on the verso other documents, very unclear, by the same hand.

title: List
summary: List of contributions (?). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Will
summary: The will of Fl. Phoibammon, chief physician of Antinoe. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Will
summary: This is the draft of 67151, written almost certainly by Dioscoros. The scribe who wrote 67151 seems to have copied this draft, without always understanding it. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract of divorce
summary: Contract of divorce between Aurelius Menas and his wife Aurelia Maria. One clause allows the two parties to remarry. The divorce is founded on mutual consent of the parties, in conformity with a recent enactment by Justin II (Nov. 2, AD 566). Belongs to to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract of divorce
summary: Contract of divorce, between Fl. Kallinikos, notary, et Aur. Kyra. The separation, amiably concluded, leaves each of the two parties their  personal goods, and the right to remarry. Neither of the parents shall be given special custody of the child born of this marriage; both shall continue to look after him. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Deed of settlement
summary: On the verso, a deed of settlement from a father to his daughter(?), leaving her his fortune in recompense for her devotion during the illness and old age of the testator, and annuling in this intent a previous settlement. This verso was without doubt written a long time after the recto: between 566 and 570, like the other papyri from Antinoe. A good example of the revocation of a mortis causa donatio. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Deed of divorce
summary: Deed of divorce between Aurelius Sarapion and his wife Aurelia Maria. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Settling of accounts
summary: Settling of accounts between Aur. Tekrompia,  anthylopratissa, and Aur. Maria her daughter. Since the death of her husband, the first named has worked and managed the fortune alone. The elder of the children, Mary, being at present married, claims her part of her father's legacy. Recapitulating receipts and expenses, the estate finds itself in deficit of 8 nomismata less 48 keratia. Mary claims from her mother the payment of a rent (1 nomisma less 6 keratia), for the part which comes to her - that is to say a quarter - of two houses previously belonging to her father and apparently occupied by the widow. In response, the widow demands of her daughter the payment of a quarter of the common debt of 8 n. less 48 k. Following the agreement they have concluded, Mary will not make this payment until after the death of her mother; she will pay her part of the debt to the estate, and undertakes to claim nothing from the moveable goods or furniture, at the beginning of this inheritance, having already received her part. She renounces even all claims on the goods which her mother might acquire, after the contract. For her, there remains only her right to the fourth part of the aforesaid houses, whose rent, while waiting, shall be paid to her each year by Aur. Tekrompia. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Duplicate of P.Cair.Masp.156 (Settling of accounts)
summary: Duplicate of 67156. Same writing: purely graphic variants. The right half is lost, but the remaining part is almost intact. This manuscript has served to fill in the missing parts of the previous one. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract of association
summary: Contract of association between two carpenters, Aurelius Psois and Aurelius Iosephis, known as Pekysis, his father in law. The profits and the losses shall be borne half to the account of each of the two parties. The text, however, is incomplete: the leftedge is missing, to a width varying between about ten and fifteen letters. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Payment of an eranos-loan
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. The text was found on two separate fragments. Large and unattractive cursive.

title: Contract of association
summary: Contract of association between two carpenters, Aurelius Daniel and Aurelius Victor. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Duplicate of previous contract (67159)
summary: Duplicate of 67159, in the same hand; almost complete, missing only a fragment towards the middle. There seems to be no variant text. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Power of attorney
summary: Power of attorney given to the lawyer Fl. Dioscoros by Aurelia Athanasia on the subject of her father's inheritance, of which she claims the half  from her uncle Theodoros. Athanasia recognises in advance everything her charge will do by virtue of the ull powers she has assigned to him, and undertakes to indemnify all expenses. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Borrowing contract
summary: Ioannes, deacon of the monastery of St Victor at Pindaros in the Antinoite nome, acknowledges having borrowed from Fl. Christophoros, a landowner at Antinoe, the sum of two nomismata, which he will return after a period of two months, without interest. Subscription in one line on the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Borrowing contract
summary: Aurelius Petros, of  Lykopolis, embroiderer, acknowledges having borrowed from Aur. Georgios, wine merchant, the sum of  7 keratia which he will return after a delay of little more than a month, on the  11 Tybi (6 January)  of the same indiction. The interest is 50 talents per month. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Borrowing contract
summary: Borrowing contract. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt
summary: Aurelius Martinos, employee in the offices of the arabarch  of Antinoe, acknowledges having paid a debt contracted with him by  Aurelius Kollouthos. The acknowledgement signed by the last named, having been mislaid by Martinos shall therefore be null if it is found later. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt of a pawnbroker. A certain Callinikos has borrowed from [...] a sum of 33 golden coins, and has given in, as a pledge of payment, (capital and interest) some objects of precious metal. He died before the redemption date, which has subsequently arrived, and the lender claims from his son Constantine the restitution of the debt. Constantine, who performs the duties of singularis to the dux of Thebaid, having been unable to discharge the debt in money, renounces the pledges handed in by his father. As for the note signed by the aforementioned, as the lender has not been able to return it, they agree that it shall be cancelled by the present receipt. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Delivery receipt
summary: A receipt issued by Theodoros, bishop of Pentapolis, to the monks of the convent (tabennesiotikos) of Pouinkoreus, in the Hermopolite nome of the Thebaid. He has previously bought from them, and paid in advance, 1500 knidia of wine, which have just been delivered to him at Alexandria. He gives them a receipt, and cancels the note signed by them, which acknowledged their debt. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Sale of land
summary: Contract of sale for an aroura of land. The beginning is lost, and only the buyer's name is known. The piece of land to be sold is located in this nome, but the contract, in the hand of Dioscoros, could possibly have been drawn up at Antinoe. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Delivery contract
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Large, clear hand, similar to that of BGU IV 1114.

title: Sale of land
summary: The beginning of the papyrus appears under 67169. There are 4 fragments, in bad condition; the last fragment joins precisely with 67169.  1st fragment: 25 x 28.6; 2nd: 16.5 x 28.6?; 3rd: 16.5 x 28.6?; 4th: 8.5 x 19. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Rental agreement (land)
summary: Two gardeners, Aurelius Senouthes and Aurelius Iulius, rent to the monastery of Zmin a vegetable garden for an unlimited time, depending on the wishes of the owners. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Heading of a contract
summary: Head of a contract agreed by [�] and the monks of the convent of Zmin. Only the beginning renains, but it seems that this papyrus is simply the duplicate of 67170. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of the Iliad
summary: Page of codex, of which there remain only two straight strips one from the side of the binding, the other from the free side of the sheet. The verses on top are entirely lost. The lower part of the fragment is, on the contrary, complete. On the recto are verses 494-519, on the verso 528-555. The codex therefore had 36 lines per page. The fragment contains accentuation added later. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of the Iliad
summary: Same codex as 67172, next page. The verses that appear here are 556-576 (recto) and 594-614 (verso).  The first three verses do not appear in their usual order, and one of them has been added later and perhaps in another hand. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of the Iliad
summary: The third page of the codex containing both 67172 and 67173.  Lines 631-641 (recto) and 667-678 (verso). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Biography of the orator Isocrates
summary: The biography was framed in a triple 'wavy' line. To the left were indicated the subdivisions of the work and the qualities which distinguish it. The fragment is not part of an edition of Isocrates, since the lines of the verso do not read in the same direction as those of the recto. The verso text is fragmentary at best. It must have been intended for use at school. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract
summary: Contract written in Coptic. The sheet has been cut to the size needed for the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Grammatical exercise
summary: On the verso, the conjugation of two Greek verbs (poiein and chrusoun) have been written. The coptic papyrus has been cut then folded, and its back has formed these two pages of Greek grammar which are complete, apart from one small lacuna, but are in a very bad state of conservation. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.  See Fournet, Hell�nisme (MIFAO 115) p. 181-203 no. III 1.

title: Poetry (an Encomium)
summary: A poem in dactylic hexametre by Dioscoros. The present piece is addressed to an unnamed person - the dux of Thebaid - in whom the author is praising, among other qualities, illustrious birth. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Documentary papyrus.
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Abraded document in very small hand.

title: Protocol
summary: A protocol in the typical protocol script of this period. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Poetry
summary: A birthday poem by Dioscoros, praising an unnamed dux of the Thebaid.  The name is not given, but the official position of the addressee is clearly indicated. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.  See Fournet, Hell�nisme (MIFAO 115) p. 422-433 no. IV 24.

title: Epithalamion
summary: An epithalamion, addressed to the illustrious comes Callinikos, fianc� to Theophile. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Epithalamion
summary: An epithalamion for the same Callinikos as 67179. We find there verses or fragments of verses used in the previous item. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Epithalamion
summary: This fragment is part of 67180. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Poetry
summary: A poem adressed to an anonymous state employee, son of a certain Hadrian. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Poetry
summary: Part of a poem addressed to the Emperor Justin II, shortly after his accession. It seems, from certain expressions, that the author is talking about the arrival of the emperor at Antinoopolis, and festivities given by the dux of Thebaid on this occasion.

title: Two poems
summary: Two poems in dactylic hexametres, written on palimpsest. They are encomia in honour of a dux of the Thebaid, perhaps Callinikos. An old contract (?) has been almost everywhere scratched out, to make a clean space. The letters which remain visible of the of the original text intersect those of the new one - which, together with the bad state of preservation of the papyrus, make a reading often very uncertain. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter
summary: Seven lines almost totally faded, representing the end of a letter, concerning a kyrios Menas, who is perhaps the pagarch of Antaiopolis (67002). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Two poems
summary: Two whole poems of Dioscoros in a bad state of conservation.  The first is addressed to Hypatios, exceptor or excubitor of the dux of Thebaid; the second to Paulos, son of Domninus, and cancellarius of the officium. The second poem is more neatly written than the first. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.  See Fournet, Hell�nisme (MIFAO 115) p. 388 no. IV 8 & 9.

title: Delivery contract
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Large, unattractive hand, partly abraded.

title: Fragment of a protocol
summary: Fragment of a protocol in typical byzantine protocol script. The first line is lost. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Four lines of poetry
summary: Four lines of verse by Dioscoros, damaged, but forming a complete piece. It appearently refers to the stay of Dioscoros in Constantinople (c.f. 67032). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros. See Fournet, Hell�nisme (MIFAO 115) p. 451 no. IV 46.

title: Protocol
summary: Protocol in typical Byzantine protocol script. The document, of five lines, may be complete(?), but is very damaged.  Here, as in 67190, we should notice that after the initial F (or Fl. in monogram) and five identical downstrokes  between them, we recognise with certainty the letters tr. On line 4 we make out D�rothe (the two first letters are hypothetical). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Poetry
summary: The verso of the document contains a poem in honour of Collouthos, pagarch of Antaiopolis (cf. 67005, 67120). The 9 first verses seem to be a discourse addressed by the town of Antaiopolis (?) to the province of Arcadia. From verse 10 on, the handwriting changes and becomes much smaller. The subject, also, seems completely different. However, no break separates these two pieces. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: The recto contains a mutilated fragment of a contract.  The verso, however, contains work of the poet of Aphrodito, Dioscoros, who has written a prayer with strong hints of Gnosticism, and an attempted poem on the games of Ancient Greece. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Poetry
summary: The recto contains a mutilated fragment of a contract.  The verso, however, contains work of Dioscoros, who has written a prayer with strong hints of Gnosticism, and a poem on the games of Ancient Greece.  Also on the verso of the papyrus, there is an ethopoieia on Apollo with Hyacinthus and Daphne. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of protocol.
summary: Fragment of protocol in typical byzantine protocol script. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: The three lines of the beginning stand alone.  The address on the verso is incomplete. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: Remains of six lines of a letter, burnt on right and left. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Fragment of a private account, burnt on right and left. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Documentary papyrus.
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Abraded document in hand different to that of the recto

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: Letter addressed from one official to another (qualified with adelphotes: is it Apollos, the father of  Dioscoros?).  The subject of the letter is a peasant, taxed in the tax rolls for lands which are not his. The topoteretes Helladios already appears in P. Beauge II.16. cf. 67023. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: The letter here is addressed to Dioscoros, containing three lines: the right half is lost. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Beginning of a letter
summary: Beginning of a letter, left half. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: Fragment of a letter of seven lines addressed to a monk - adelphotes. Only the left half remains. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: Fragment of a letter addressed to a monk. Burnt right and left. It concerns, like 67070, golden coins (olokotina) of insufficient weight. The hand appears to be the same as 67193.  However, the two fragments do not seem part of the same document. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragments of a letter
summary: Fragments of a letter addressed to the protocometes of Aphrodito. Address on the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: Fragment of an administrative letter.  Dioscoros, son of Apollos, has been included in a list of taxpayers. The comes (Ammonios?), protector of his family, (cf. 67062; 67138) has already had him removed from the list. He has once again been added, by order of the protocometes (?) Herakleios (cf. 67055 recto) and his colleagues. The employee responsible for the addition apologises to comes Ammonios. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter
summary: A letter of five lines, complete, but partially illegible. Proper names: Nonna daughter of Nemesianos, Asklepios son of Dorotheos and kyrios Dioscoros. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter
summary: Letter addressed by an official to Apollos or to Dioscoros, his son, on the subject of a woman who has been kept in prison (cf. 67078), and to whom the praeses has, in vain, ordered her discharge. The address on the verso of the document is incomplete. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter
summary: The letter is complete, but the writing has, for the greater part, faded. According to the address on the verso, the letter is sent by a certain Collouthos to Apollos the protocometes. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Loan with deposit
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Medium-sized , clear cursive; partly badly preserved. On the verso, in a different hand, BGU IV 1132.

title: Fragment of a letter (?)
summary: Fragment of a letter (?). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragments of a petition
summary: Small fragments forming the end of a petition to a nameless official, who must be the dux of Thebaid. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Financial account
summary: Written by the steward of comes Ammonios. There is a list of proper names in which we find some names known thanks to 67138-9. In the margin a supplementary account. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Fragment of a private account, very mutilated. The large round cursive resembles that of Dioscoros. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Fragment of a private account.  Almost all that remains is the column of figures. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Account (private?)
summary: Fragment of an account, possibly private, badly mutilated. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: A fragment of a private account, which is, apparently, illegible. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private register
summary: Scanty remains of one sheet, and the margin of the previous sheet. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Fragment of a private account, completely corroded by the sebakh. Opisthographon, but entirely illegible on both sides. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Fragment of a private account; to a large part anepigraphon. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter concerning payment for croton
summary: Letter of an official to Euphranor, calling to his attention a hypomn�ma received from the basilikoi ge�rgoi of the village of Philopator, a copy of which is appended. [=Chrest.Wilck. 304]

title: Payment of a loan with deposit
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Same hand as BGU IV 1116, but larger and more hasty. Contains the drafts of two documents; on the recto BGU IV 1118.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Almost completely faded. The round cursive resembles that of Dioscoros. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Fragment of a private account, apparently belonging to the archives of comes Ammonios. Only a few words remain. Similar cursive as in 67138 and 67139. The papyrus comes from the page of a book: it bears on the verso the remains of two lines of writing, and two designs in the form of foliage. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Fragment of a private account. Perhaps this is part of the same document as 67219, but the two pieces do not join. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: List
summary: On the verso a list of  individuals, frequently qualified  with the name of a profession - stratiotes, chrysochoos, chalkotypos, bapheus. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a register
summary: Fragment of a register similar to those of comes Ammonios. Traces remain, unfortunately insignificant, of 17 pages folded one in another. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Fragment of a private account, containing only one mutilated column of amounts. On the verso, some lines of figures. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Two fragments of an account
summary: Two fragments of an account, cut up irregularly by insects. The two pieces represent the remains of two consecutive sheets, and have identical shapes. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Five fragments of a private account
summary: Five fragments of a private account, which do not join. Also fragmentary remains of several columns, unfortunately not usable. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Fragment of a private account, almost entirely corroded by the sebakh. Perhaps to be connected with 67214. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Fragment of a private account. Similar hand to 67141 and 67142. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Payment of a loan with deposit
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Hand similar to BGU IV 1155. On the recto contents of BGU IV 1134.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: End of a sheet containing a private account. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: Fragment of a private account. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt
summary: Tax receipt for 24 keratia.  Only the signature is completely preserved, which dates the document to the 19th of Hathyr and gives the name of Collouthos the hypodectes. It is followed by another receipt (15 keratia?) for the diagraphon. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt relating to the annona militaris, issued to the protocometes of Aphrodito. This receipt is similar to those published above (67050, 67051, 67137). The formula used is the usual one for this type of document. On the verso, the beginning of the address is visible. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a receipt
summary: Fragment of a receipt similar to 67229. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a receipt
summary: Preceded by the number cmg. The text is, on the whole, damaged and has almost faded.  We may still make out the name of Phoibammon pronoetes in line 1. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Acknowledgement of a debt
summary: Acknowledgement of a debt and settlement of security with a creditor, by Dioscoros, son of Apollos. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragments of a private receipt
summary: Fragments of a private receipt (plerotike apoche). The receipt relates to the payment of certain dues (ekphoria). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: Fragments of a contract (eggraphos homologia) drawn up by Kyros (nomikos). Signatures of witnesses, but only the name of Ioannes son of Charisios is preserved. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Report of Chaff Collectors
summary: A report of chaff and hay delivered by Seuthes and Isidorus, chaff collectors of Karanis in 310/311.

title: Conclusion of contract with a wet nurse; payment of a loan with paramon�
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Top part is missing. Small cursive with many abbreviations. On the verso two documents, one under the other. On the recto, in a different hand, BGU IV 1156.

title: Fragments of a contract for the rent of land
summary: Fragment of a contract to rent land (misthosis).  There is a stipulation that fees must be in corn and in barley. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragments of a contract for the rent of land
summary: Fragment of land rental (note the word �permobaliva sic). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract for the rent of land
summary: Fragment of a contract for the rental of land (misthosis).  Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract for the rent of land
summary: Fragment of a contract of misthosis. The contract specifies a period of rental of two years, of a piece of land situated in the kleros of Treges. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragments of a contract for the rent of land
summary: Fragment of a contract of misthosis. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a land rental contract (?)
summary: Note the expression idiois anal�masi te kai autourg�masi.  The papyrus has almost entirely faded. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a document
summary: The verso of the fragment is part of an unrecognizable document of which only a few words are preserved. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a deed of sale of land
summary: Fragment of a deed of sale of land. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract for the sale of land or a house
summary: Fragment of a contract for the sale of land or a house. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: On the verso, the remains of a private account. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Loan contract
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Medium-sized hand, letters very close together, and therefore at times unclear. On the verso loan contract from 13 BC.

title: Two fragments of a document
summary: Two fragments, appearing to be parts of the same document: a deed similar to 67117 and 67119. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a rental deed
summary: Fragment of a rental deed, relating to a house (?). The writing, similar to that of  67159 and 67160, seems to indicate provenance from Antinoopolis. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Twelve fragments of a deed for a gift of land
summary: Twelve fragments, not  joining up, of a deed for the gift of land (the verb d�rein is repeated twice), in favour of a monastery, as the term to psuch�pheles, which is found on one of the pieces, seems to indicate. According to the beginning of the document, the period seems to be the reign of Justin II; a later date is not very probable. The town of Antinoopolis is mentioned; perhaps this is where it was drawn up. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: The end of a deposition
summary: End of a deposition (ekmarturion), c.f. 67087 and 67088. At the foot there is the signature by Fl. Paulos, followed, after a blank space, by the date. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Three fragments of a contract
summary: Three irregular fragments of a contract. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: A contract concerning land-rental(?) for a period of ten years. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragments of a rental contract
summary: Two fragments of a misthosis. Signed by Sansneus son of Isaakios, written by Abrahamios. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of an unspecified document (letter?)
summary: The document is very damaged.  Line 15 mentions the city of Alexandria. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a private account
summary: On the verso of the fragment are the traces of private account, and some lines written perpendicularly, which seem to be continuation of the recto. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: Fragment of a contract. Only the signature remains, preserving the name of Isaakios nomikos. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Loan contract
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. On the recto loan contract from 15 BC.

title: Fragment of a pittakion
summary: Fragment of a pittakion preserving the signatures of witnesses, Phoibammon and Aur. Papnouthios. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: A contract that is probably a misth�sis. Signatures of [�] son of Collouthos, in  the name of one of the contracting parties, and Pheib son of Isakios, who appears to have been a witness. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: Fragment of a contract, perhaps for a sale.  The words ebebai�sato and presbuterou can be made out. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: Fragment of a contract. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of an unrecognisable document
summary: The document is mutilated and half of it is faded. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: The contract contains the name Conon. The writing seems to be that of Dioscoros. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: Fragment of a contract. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Two fragments of an unspecified document
summary: The two fragments are of similar dimensions. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: Fragment of a contract  between a landowner and some nightwatchmen, who undertake to protect his property. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fourteen fragments of a register of accounts
summary: The fragments are very mutilated and unusable. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Loan contract
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Large, squiggly hand. Occasional ommission of letters, not explicable as abbreviation. On the verso is BGU IV 1153.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: The contract is in coptic. The hand is similar to that of 67176, and this piece perhaps formed part of this document. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Grammatical exercise
summary: On the verso, some forms of the verb bo�n serving as an example for -a contracted verbs. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a document
summary: Fragment of papyrus almost entirely faded. The writing of Dioscoros? Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: The document is made of four lines crossed out by chiasmos. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment (anepigraphon)
summary: Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Petition
summary: Apollos, of the village of  Phoukis in the Antaiopolite nome, tenant farmer of the heirs of comes Phoibammon, complains of having been forced by the topot�r�t�s of Antinoopolis to pay taxes for which they should not be responsible. Written by Dioscoros; the fragment is missing a strip on the left, which had been found separately and published as 67010. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Poetry (Encomium)
summary: The poetry on the verso of this document is by Dioscoros. The poem represents an encomium on unnamed officials. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Order from the praeses of the Thebaid
summary: Order (prostagma) from the praeses of the Thebaid to the people of  Aphrodito, relating to the annona. This document is similar to 67030, but it carries a different date. Unfortunately the name of the praeses (komes kai archon) has disappeared, along with all the first line. Subscription on the verso, addressing the document to the people of Aphrodito. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Order
summary: Order (prostagma) of Dioscoros, praeses of Thebaid, to Apollos son of Isaac, charging him to accept the post of riparios of Aphrodito. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Copy of a commonitorium
summary: Duplicate of a notice (commonitorium) given by the offices of the praeses of  lower Thebaid to an unnamed official, perhaps the singularis Menas (topoteretes of Antaiopolis?).  The subject is an accused person who should be arrested and who the deliberate negligence of the people of the village has left at liberty. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Petition to the strategus
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Clear cursive with occasional punctuation.

title: Report
summary: Report  (didaskalia) addressed to the Empress Theodora by the inhabitants of  Aphrodito, against the intrigues of the pagarch of Antaiopolis. The text seems to have been written by Dioscoros. The papyrus is made of three pages separated by ample margins;

title: Tax receipt
summary: Tax receipt issued to the inhabitants of Aphrodito, for the payment of 2 nomismata, allocated to the salary of the commentariensis (c.f. 67040). Subscription on the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Documentary papyrus.
summary: A few lines from the end of an entagion, written along the fibres. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros. (In the Maspero edition this is referred to as verso).

title: Tax receipts
summary: Tax receipt, issued by Ioannes, hypodectes of Aphrodito, to the house of Dioscoros Psimanobet. The text is written on the verso of an older account, accross the fibres, in which a rectangular piece, almost regular, has been cut out. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros. (In the Maspero edition this is referred to as recto).

title: Two receipts for dues in kind
summary: Two receipts for dues in kind, due by the village of Aphrodito to the convent of the Penitence (Metanoia). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Table of taxes and expenses
summary: Two fragments that do not join. Table of taxes and expenses of the town of Aphrodito, during the first indiction. The first column registers the sums spent for the kanonika and the military annona various expenses, notably salaries (synetheiai) of the officials; the following columns contain the enumeration of details of various expenses. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: List of taxpayers
summary: List of taxpayers emerging from the town of Aphrodito. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Private account
summary: Taxes due and expenses of Ioannes, a settler of a place called Ko[�] in the Lycopolite nome. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Administrative letter
summary: An employee of the office of the praeses(?) makes known to the protocometes Apollos that people in high places are unhappy with him, because he has not stopped a certain Eudoxios from abusing his power. The reconstructions of this incomplete piece are presented with all reservation. Address in one line on the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Administrative letter
summary: This letter, addressed to Taurinos, singularis of the presidal office, by a superior employee of the same office. The village of Aphrodito should benefit from a tax exemption, which it has claimed. The end is unclear, due to missing parts of the text. The address on the verso of the document notes Taurinos, and his position as singularis. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Petition
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Clear cursive, partly very abraded.

title: Fragment of an administrative letter
summary: The letter, of which this fragment is part, was addressed to Apollos, protocometes of Aphrodito; in all likelihood, a little of the text on either side is missing. On the verso, the letter is addressed to Apollos the protocometes by Ioannes, who is perhaps the pagarch of Antaiopolis who signed several of the receipts of 67325. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: It is possible that this letter was addressed to the protocometes of Aphrodito. The address on the verso is incomplete, and thus, any definitive answer regarding the addressees of this letter is tentative. However, the use of the term eudokimotatos seems to imply that the officials in question were protocometes (cf. 67291 verso). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Duplicate of 67089
summary: Duplicate of 67089. Two groups of fragments, between which two lines are missing altogether. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Collection of four various parts
summary: This collection is the work of a copyist who was writing in the second half of the 6th century. This may explain the errors made by the scribe, errors which appear particularly numerous in document no. 2. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.
date_text: Second half VIth Century AD (Copy of col. I-II made towards 566-570)

title: Deed of surety
summary: Deed of surety, given to the riparios Apollos by the deacon  Anouphis, and an officer of the garrison of  Antaiopolis, on account of two workmen. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Deed of surety
summary: Deed of surety dated the same day as P.Cair.Masp. III.67296, but probably in a different hand. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract of emphyteusis
summary: Contract of  emphyteusis, between a certain Senouthes, clarissimus, and the church of Tentyra (Denderah).  However, it is more probable that the contract comes from Antinoopolis. Senouthes would be an inhabitant of this last town, and could have properties in distant nomes. The verso is entirely covered in tachygraphic signs, which are interrupted numerous times to give place to a few discontinued words. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract of emphyteusis
summary: The monastery of Saint Phoibammon sells to the lawyer  Phoibammon, on 'emphyteutic' lease, a cultivated field (psilon chorema, in contrast to built up land), with its dependencies, a cowshed and two kellia. The annual rent goes up to  19 keratia. On the erso, tachygraphic signs, similar to those which cover the verso of 67298. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Rental of land
summary: Sibylle and Herais, acting in the name of Sourous(?), have granted to Victor, son of Papnouthes, for a period of three years, a property situated in the kleros Ouesotos.  He undertakes to pay the taxes, in cash and in kind (chrysika, annona and naula), d to pay to the owners fee of 28 artabai of corn, 50 cheeses, four measures of vegetables in brine and a third of a golden coin. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Lease of a part of a house
summary: Lease of a part of a house by Aurelia Ama Rachel (?) to Isaac her brother. On the verso, text in tachygraphic characters. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter of Isidora to Asklepiades
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. The back is blank.

title: Lease of a wagon
summary: Lease of a wagon for the transport of a crop. The owner of the wagon is Dioscoros, son of Apollos, and the papyrus proves that he still lived at Aphrodito after the year 552. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a contract
summary: The contract refers to the employment of a steward. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract
summary: Contract of apprenticeship, and of board and lodging. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Reicept for a debt
summary: Receipt for a debt of 5 nomismata, and cancellation of the acknowledgement, which the creditor has not been able to find to return it to the debitor. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt for dues in kind and money
summary: This receipt was paid by Apollos, son of Dioscoros, tenant of a piece of land belonging to the Church of Aphrodito. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Acknowledgement of a debt (asphaleia)
summary: Sansneous, son of Isac, has borrowed from Apollos two artabai of corn and two of barley, for the sowing of his field. The verso contains a subscription in a single line. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract of marriage
summary: This document is a contract of marriage between two unnamed individuals.  The form of the contract is somewhat irregular: it has been drawn up after the consummation of the marriage. The verso of the document seems to be a continuation of the marriage contract that appears on the recto of the document. Line 9, verso: suzugias sumph�non �toi gamikon sumbolaion. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract of Divorce
summary: The names of the two parties are lost: the clauses are similar to those in 67121, 67153, 67155. Dioscoros arrived at Antinoopolis in AD 566.  Following the restoration [trit�]s in line 1, the date is AD 569-70. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Will of Fl. Theodoros
summary: This document represents the will of Flavius Theodoros, employed in the offices of the dux of the Thebaid.  Cf. 67151, it is similar style, often the same phraseology, and has a long drawn out quality.  The document is incomplete. The testator, having no children, divides his fortune between three heirs: to the monastery of Saint Senouthios at Atripe, he leaves all his property located in the Hermopolite, Antinoite and Panopolite nomes or elsewhere, just as in the towns of Hermopolis and Antinoe, charging that the revenues from there be used for 'pious' works. To the convent of Apa Mousaios he leaves all his movable goods. To his grandmother he leaves an unspecified property. His slaves shall be freed. Two fragments, separated by a small lacuna (l. 78); the end is missing. The verso is occupied by a text in tachygraphic characters. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Division of inheritance
summary: Division of the inheritance of two brothers, Philemon and [...]. The first and only remaining part, consists of the terms on the part of Philemon: first, the field called  Apalasias, on the territory of the village of Arabon, with its dependencies and its islands; second, a house situated in this same town; third, two slaves, a brother and sister, and their future descendants. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Two letters of Isidora to Asklepiades
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Three columns, the first only contains a few remains. The two complete columns contain the whole second letter.

title: Division of inheritance
summary: Division of an inheritance between a widow and her five sons. The five sons have inherited from their father, but are bound to certain obligations to their mother: g�roboskeisthai and nosokomeisthai in case of illness, (cf. 67097) paying the interest on a debt which she has, and lastly to pay her an income which the deceased has left (which is designated by the curious expression et�sion pekoulion. The hand is that of Dioscoros. The document is in three fragments, that do not join. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Contract of association
summary: This is the third example of a contract already known as 67158 and 67159. The hand is that of Dioscoros. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Poem (an Encomium)
summary: On the verso, a poem of Dioscoros to the dux of the Thebaid, Callinicus. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Protocol
summary: On the recto, five lines of large writing, reproducing a protocol similar to those which have appeared earlier, notably 67151. It is however less stylised, and therefore more legible, and would function as a valuable aid to the decipherment of such scripts, if only it were not in such a hopeless state of conservation. It consists of several small fragments, which could be re-arranged thanks to the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Two poems by Dioscoros
summary: On the verso, two poems by Dioscoros: the first takes the subject of the death of Achilles (Dioscoros has adopted the legend according to which Achilles, lured into an ambush, would have been killed at the moment he celebrated his marriage with Polyxene) and is an ethopoieia; the second poem is addressed to comes Domninos cancellarius on the prefectural staff. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a poem by Dioscoros
summary: This poem, by Dioscoros, is in iambic trimetres.  The subject seems to be the praise of a dux of the Thebaid. Typical hand of Dioscoros. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Poetry
summary: Poem in Homeric hexametres, the work of Dioscoros: epithalamion of a certain Isaac (Isakios) (cf. 67179). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: List of pastures
summary: List of pastures (bosk�mata) belonging to Dioscoros, son of Apollos, and that have been laid waste by his enemies. On the sense of the word bosk�ma, cf. 67097 (A).2; 67112.11.  As we already possess a list of the same nature, (67143) written in the hand of Dioscoros, it is possible that this also is concerned with him (although his name can be read with that of his grandfather Dioscoros Psimanobet). The attacks against his property would then be the result of the measures taken against him by the pagarch of Antaiopolis in the year 566 (67002) and which forced him to flee to Antinoe. This devastation no doubt consisted of having bestial strangers on his grazing land (cf. 67002). On the verso, there is a long subscription which has almost entirely faded. We may still read the first word and the last ones. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Order
summary: Order of the praeses of the lower Thebaid, Thomas, relating to the annona of the Garrison of  Antaiopolis, for the fifth indiction. The hand of this document is similar to that of 67030, 67280 and 67281. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter
summary: Letter of Ioannes (pagarch of Antaiopolis?) to a protocometes of Aphrodito. The subscription on the verso of the document is made up of two lines. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Property declarations
summary: Three property declarations addressed to the epimeletes. They give a brief specification of the property and an assessment of its value. All three are made out in the month Phaophi. [=Chrest.Wilck. 224a-c]

title: Edict of Aristius Optatus
summary: Aristius Optatus, a prefect of Egypt who is otherwise unknown, issued this edict on Phamenoth 20, to publicize and give effect to an edict previously issued by Diocletian in the name of the tetrarchs which introduced important changes in the tax system of Egypt.

title: Letter
summary: This letter is addressed to Apollos, a protocometes of Aphrodito, from Psaios, numerarius, employed in the offices of the praeses(?). Comes  Ammonios appeared already in 67062, 67138 and 67139. His title and his role here seem to prove that he was comes kai archon, that is to say, the praeses of the lower Thebaid. The subscription on the verso of the document notes both the name of the addressee, Apollos, and his position, as well as the sender, Psios. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: End of a will
summary: The end of the will of Aurelius Pankab, son of  Panouphis. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Inventory
summary: On the verso an inventory of movable property and furniture, in coptic. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Book of accounts
summary: The book is composed of eight leaves bound together by a thread, and contains documents of different sorts: the first relates to the administration of  an agricultural estate. The second, receipts for taxes (embole; demosia; naula) paid by a certain Cornelius, son of Philantinoos. The third, similar receipts, issued to Dioscoros. The fourth, a deed of rental of land belonging to a widow, daughter of Ioannes son of Cornelius. The script accounts appears to be that of Dioscoros; the tax receipts are in several other hands. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.
date_text: VIth Century AD (Fol. IV, verso Fol. II: AD 585; verso Fol IV: AD 585-86)

title: Receipt for rent
summary: The heirs of Kyros, represented by Christodoros, have received from Apollos, their tenant, the sum of the fees owed by him for the field situated at a place called  Piase, on the territory of the village of  Phthla. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Series of receipts
